title: Compound emojis rendered as separate components
body: ### Describe the bug Compound emojis like 👩‍🔬 woman scientist consisting of three Unicode code points 👩U+1F469 ‍U+200D 🔬U+1F52C is displayed as 👩<200d>🔬 in zsh. ### Steps to reproduce font: tried both macOS terminal default font and Source Code Pro emoji font: system default Apple Color Emoji On my system, compound emojis like👩‍🔬 and 👨‍👨‍👧‍👧 etc are displayed normally in VS Code and web pages. You can reproduce this issue in zsh by pasting the woman scientist 👩‍🔬 emoji onto the terminal and observe that it automatically expands into 👩<200d>🔬. ### Expected behavior Should be displayed as a whole, rather than decomposed into individual components and a Zero Width Joiner. ### Screenshots and recordings Terminal.app with macOS default terminal font: VSCode terminal with Source Code Pro font: iTerm (fresh install): ### OS / Linux distribution macOS Monterey 12.1 ### Zsh version 5.8 ### Terminal emulator tried both Terminal.app and iTerm2 ### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_
author: AlienKevin

title: iw executable cannot be called after installing ohmyzsh
body:
### Describe the bug

Hi, on a Raspberry Pi running _Raspbian GNU/Linux 11 (bullseye)_, the `pi` user can successfully execute `iw dev` before ohmyzsh installation.
After installation, the same fails:

```
➜  ~ iw dev
zsh: command not found: iw
```

Calling the executable by absolute path still works: 

```
➜  ~ /usr/sbin/iw dev
phy#2
        Interface wlan0
                ifindex 3
[...]
```

When running bash, calling `iw dev` also still works.

So it does not seem to be a problem with the executable, but rather zsh cannot locate it for some reason.

### Steps to reproduce

Try calling `iw dev` as unprivileged user. Install ohmyzsh using install script. Try command again.

### Expected behavior

`iw dev` should work with zsh like it does with bash.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Raspbian GNU/Linux 11 (bullseye)

### Zsh version

5.8

### Terminal emulator

SSH session in Konsole

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

_No response_

title: bureau.zsh-theme/bureau_git_prompt() Using Reserved Variable name with latest update
body:
### Describe the bug

The issue is confirmed to be cross-platform (occurs in every linux box/VM i tried it in)

bureau.zsh-theme/bureau_git_prompt() (https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/themes/bureau.zsh-theme#L69) uses an apparently "read-only" variable name (`$status`)

This causes the function to always fail, and print the line `bureau_git_prompt:2: read-only variable: status` every time it's called, screwing up the theme quite a bit.

author: 0cyn

title: (kubectx): quote % in `kubectx_prompt_info` breaks coloring
body:
### Describe the bug

[This commit](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/commit/d87ab251c7fe18626b2d0c4e4a184e7bed7c508b) introduced quoting of %, but it is unclear to me why (@mcornella who authored the change might be able to shed some light on that). This causes the cursor placement as well as alignment to break when using a colored value in the associative array `kubectx_mapping` for the right side prompt.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable plugin kubectx
1. Add the following to `.zshrc`:
```
RPS1='$(kubectx_prompt_info)'
kubectx_mapping[my_kube_ctx]="%{$fg[green]%}My Context%{$reset_color%}"
```
1. Reload `.zshrc`
1. Switch to the `my_kube_ctx` context

The cursor is now too far left and the kubectx info is not fully right aligned and also contains additional %{} characters. If the `%{...%}` indicators are removed from the `kubectx_mapping` the additional characters go away but the cursor is still messed up and the kubectx info is still incorrectly aligned.

### Expected behavior

The cursor is after the left side prompt and the kubectx info is fully right aligned.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Ubuntu 20.04

### Zsh version

5.8

### Terminal emulator

Windows Terminal

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

WSL2

### Additional context

_No response_

title: [yarn plugin] Yarn completion is not working accordingly
body:
### Describe the bug

Before #10516 I was able to run completion for `scripts` without having to add the `run` command.

### Steps to reproduce

Let's say I have a script called `start-dev`. Typing `yarn s` and pressing TAB was completing `start-dev` successfully. After #10516 it does not work anymore. I need to type `yarn run s` and press TAB to make it work.

Not sure why the two snippets below aren't using the same logic:
https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/3a1d83d778388b574bde70f41e9674092dafe6ef/plugins/yarn/_yarn#L119
https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/3a1d83d778388b574bde70f41e9674092dafe6ef/plugins/yarn/_yarn#L147

But I was able to fix it locally using the same logic of line 147 at line 119, that would be:
```bash
scripts=($(cat "${packageJson}" | perl -0777 -MJSON::PP -n -E '%r=%{decode_json($_)->{scripts}}; printf "$_:$r{$_}\n" for sort keys %r'))
```

After this change, I'm able to type `yarn s` and press TAB to make `yarn start-dev` completion.

### Expected behavior

Completion should work when typing `yarn <script>` without the `run` command. 

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

Garuda Linux

5.8

master (9c84c344)

author: djfinnoy

title: Deprecated update settings take precedence over `zstyle` ones
body:
### Describe the bug

With both
```zsh
zstyle ':omz:update' mode disabled
```
and
```zsh
DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT=true
```
the check for update script continues the update, though the `zstyle` setting tells it to disable it.

The `zstyle` setting should have precedence, as the other ones are deprecated. 

The setting was set by powerlevel10k (_Originally posted by @Mystic8b in https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/discussions/10584#discussioncomment-1963679_):

> It looks like the problem is in p10k.
> ```
> ❯ grep -rni "DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT" .
> ./.oh-my-zsh/custom/themes/powerlevel10k/internal/p10k.zsh:6336:  DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT=true
> ```

### Steps to reproduce

Set in your zshrc file:
```sh
zstyle ':omz:update' mode disabled
DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT=true
```

### Expected behavior

The update script stops the check since the `zstyle` setting is newer and should take precedence.

### Screenshots and recordings


```sh
# Get user's update preferences
#
# Supported update modes:
# - prompt (default): the user is asked before updating when it's time to update
# - auto: the update is performed automatically when it's time
# - reminder: a reminder is shown to the user when it's time to update
# - disabled: automatic update is turned off
zstyle -s ':omz:update' mode update_mode || update_mode=prompt
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:14> zstyle -s :omz:update mode update_mode

# Support old-style settings
[[ "$DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT" != true ]] || update_mode=auto
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:17> [[ true != true ]]
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:17> update_mode=auto
[[ "$DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE" != true ]] || update_mode=disabled
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:18> [[ '' != true ]]

# Cancel update if:
# - the automatic update is disabled.
# - the current user doesn't have write permissions nor owns the $ZSH directory.
# - git is unavailable on the system.
if [[ "$update_mode" = disabled ]] \
   || [[ ! -w "$ZSH" || ! -O "$ZSH" ]] \
   || ! command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  unset update_mode
  echo cancelled && set +xv
  return
fi
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:24> [[ auto = disabled ]]
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:25> [[ ! -w /home/user/.oh-my-zsh || ! -O /home/user/.oh-my-zsh ]]
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:26> command -v git
echo continued && set +xv
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:31> echo continued
continued
+/home/user/.oh-my-zsh/tools/check_for_upgrade.sh:31> set +xv
~
```

_Originally posted by @Mystic8b in https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/discussions/10584#discussioncomment-1963487_


### OS / Linux distribution

N/A

### Zsh version

N/A

### Oh My Zsh version

master (aaebe4c8)

### Terminal emulator

N/A

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: svn plugin broken svn_get_rev_nr after 93ec48fb0a798775b9e5d366c11a4ac3826c3ea4
body:
### Describe the bug

Commit 93ec48fb0a798775b9e5d366c11a4ac3826c3ea4 (@mcornella) changed svn_get_rev_nr but there is a sed error, as sed expect reading from stdin or file name, I think there should be
`sed -n 's/Revision:\ //p' <<<"${1:-$(LANG= svn info 2>/dev/null)}"`
not
`sed -n 's/Revision:\ //p' "${1:-$(LANG= svn info 2>/dev/null)}"`
At least this change fix my setup

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable plugin with svn support with revision number.
2. cd to svn directory
3. got error, that sed can't read from (svn info output) and no revision number in prompt.

### Expected behavior

Revision number in prompt.

### Describe the bug

I know this behaviour has been reported a numerous times but I', not sure if mine is related to the config of the other reports - anyway: 
I have a not that complex ssh config, but when typing ssh/scp and a first letter then hitting tab it takes 4-5s to complete, which is imho unusable. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. `ssh a`
2. hit tab
3. wait...

My zshrc looks like this:

```
export ZSH="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh"
ZSH_THEME="robbyrussell"
export NVM_LAZY_LOAD=true
export NVM_COMPLETION=true
plugins=(git zsh-syntax-highlighting docker zsh-nvm npm docker-compose evalcache)
source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh
_evalcache rbenv init -
zstyle ':completion:*' hosts off
```
Had the powerlevel10k theme and changed that to the default one, which resulted in no change. Just commenting out the oh-my-zsh.sh helps :(

Completion should be done within a blink of an eye

_No response_

master (1e27755)

iTerm2

### Additional context

My ssh config is fairly simple:

```
➜  .ssh cat config
Include ~/.ssh/conf.d/*

I have 4 files in `conf.d` which have all Host / Hostname configurations. The overall number of HostName entries is 296. 
All configurations are flat, except one file which has the following pattern:

```
Host node-1
    node-1.fqdn.com

Host node-2
    node-2.fqdn.com

Host node-3
    node-3.fqdn.com

Host node-n

Host node-1 node-2 node-3 ... node-N

    Port 1234
```

### Describe the bug

Hi, I have installed ohmyzsh from GitHub on rocky linux 8.5 and when I use the tab key to complete, the first character is repeated.
For example, if I type in:

```
~ sys
```

then tab, it turns to

```
~ ssys
sysctl                          systemd-cgtop                   systemd-resolve               
syserror                        systemd-delta                   systemd-run      
...
```
And the first 's' cannot be deleted by backspace.
The ssh software I use is Xshell on Windows.
When I use CentOS 7, this bug does not appear.
It seems to be a problem with agnoster theme under rocky linux, using robbyrussell theme did not find this bug.
I have searched for such issues and configured them.
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/19305291/remnant-characters-when-tab-completing-with-zsh

export TERM=xterm-256color
export LANG="en_US.UTF-8"

ZSH_THEME="agnoster"

plugins=(

)

title: Postgres plugin: uses wrong Homebrew location for Apple Silicon
body:
### Describe the bug

Homebrew on Apple Silicon is by default installed into `/opt/homebrew`, while the plugin uses the Intel location.
https://docs.brew.sh/Installation

This causes almost all PostgreSQL aliases to fail on Apple Silicon as the now default initialized location for a new database is:
`initdb --locale=C -E UTF-8 /opt/homebrew/var/postgres`

Maybe it's possible to support both? or create a intel/silicon plugin version?

Thank you for the great work :)

### Steps to reproduce

**On Apple Silicon Mac, install Homebrew + Oh My Zsh**

Enable plugin postgres
Reload terminal/.zshrc file
Execute: `startpost`

I expect the PostgreSQL database to start, however get this message:

title: Option to disable adding yarn global scripts to `$PATH`
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

`yarn` plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

See https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/issues/10290#issuecomment-1026504871

Tl;dr: `yarn global bin` takes a long time (`yarn global bin  0.15s user 0.05s system 83% cpu 0.241 total` on my system), adding significant overhead to new sessions.

Additionally, Yarn v2 and later doesn't support the command, so in projects using the modern versions of Yarn the command always fails. Using `hyperfine`, calling `zsh -i -c exit` takes ` 1.568 s ±  0.063 s` with the yarn plugin and `298.8 ms ±   6.6 ms` with it removed.

Also of note is that the global `yarn` will probably remain on v1 forever (see e.g. https://github.com/nodejs/docker-node/issues/1180#issuecomment-578825071 for some discussion about which version to ship in the office `node` Docker images)

### Describe the proposed feature.

Add some way of disabling the feature added in #9410. Probably some environment variable?

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Disabling the plugin entirely. But I dearly love the aliases and completions, so that would be a shame 🙂

_No response_

### Related Issues

#10290, #10396
author: SimenB

title: Infinite recursion when setting theme
body:
### Describe the bug

I'm not sure if this is a bug worth fixing, but I'll post it here in case anyone else comes across it.
I'd also gladly make a pull request to fix it if needed.

The bug happens with the command `omz theme set [theme]` being run inside of a script. Specifically, this is caused by line 737 in the file `lib/cli.zsh` where omz restarts the shell causing the script to run from the start and loop infinitely. I understand that that line basically is used to load the new theme, but restarting the shell is really only permissible when running on the terminal directly.

I've fixed this in my script using a hacky workaround that prevents this sort of recursion:
```
#!/bin/zsh

# Check if this has been run before
# to prevent infinite recursion
if [[ count -gt 0 ]]
then
    echo "[COMPLETE] Theme set!"
    exit 0
fi
export count=$(expr $count + 1)

# source needed files
source /usr/share/oh-my-zsh/zshrc

# Set the theme
omz theme set fino &

# Commands from this point on will run
```

### Steps to reproduce

Run the following in a script:

```
#!/bin/zsh

source /usr/share/oh-my-zsh/zshrc

omz theme set fino

# Commands from this point on will not run
```

Though it holds true for any theme being set

### Expected behavior

Expected behavior: The script runs once without any looping

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Artix Linux

### Zsh version

5.6

### Terminal emulator

urxvt

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_
author: OliveThePuffin

title: RFC: Add option to disable ohmyzsh aliases
body:
Hi everyone!

It's a common sentiment to feel that omz adds too many aliases in different areas, such as `git` ones (discussed in #5721), default omz ones (#9414) or others. There's a pull request actually (#10510) that fix this issue, but it uses environment variables (not recommended actually, as it pollutes the global variable namespace) and is not granular, as you only can disable all default omz aliases and all plugin aliases.

I would like to ask you what do you think about this solution, as I think that is cleaner:

Plugin aliases would be disabled by setting the following snippet in your `.zshrc`:

```zsh
zstyle ':omz:plugins:<nameofplugin>' alias off
```

For instance:

```zsh
zstyle ':omz:plugins:yarn' alias off
```

They can also be disabled all at once, using wildcards:

```zsh
zstyle ':omz:plugins:*' alias off
```

And enable only a few plugins:

```zsh
zstyle ':omz:plugins:*' alias off
zstyle ':omz:plugins:yarn' alias on
zstyle ':omz:plugins:git' alias on
```

For default ohmyzsh aliases we could use a similar way of doing it, and disabling the whole set of aliases would be something similar to:

```zsh
zstyle ':omz:*:*' alias off
```

What do you think about this idea? Let's discuss it here!

author: ghost

title: Pre- and post- execution point files
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I had to completely redo my ohmyzsh and powerlevel10k setups the other day when my cat decided to attack my keyboard and mess up several configuration files. In doing so, it was rather difficult to parse out where I had put all my different customizations and what was just boilerplate for different tools/frameworks/themes/etc.

It would be very nice if users were able to have dedicated files that aren't ever touched by the tool, but allow the user to completely contain their customizations.

To this end, I propose pre-execution and post-execution files

### Describe the proposed feature.

Allow for `.zshrc.pre`, `.zshrc.post`, `.oh-my-zsh.pre.sh`, and `.oh-my-zsh.post.sh` scripts to be sourced at the appropriate  points based on the filename.

For instance, I have edited my `.zshrc` file to start with the following line
`[[ -f "${HOME}/.zshrc.pre" ]] && source "${HOME}/.zshrc.pre"`

And the last line in my `.zshrc` file to be this following line
`[[ -f "${HOME}/.zshrc.post" ]] && source "${HOME}/.zshrc.post"`

And I modified the line `source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh` to be the following
```
[[ -f "${HOME}/.oh-my-zsh.pre.sh" ]] && source "${HOME}/.oh-my-zsh.pre.sh"

source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh

[[ -f "${HOME}/.oh-my-zsh.post.sh" ]] && source "${HOME}/.oh-my-zsh.post.sh"
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I'm not sure what other alternatives there are, but I am open to suggestions. The goal is to be able to have dedicated user configuration files specific to the framework.

### Additional context

For example, I have the following file contents in my setup

### .zshrc.pre
```
# Uncomment this line to enable zsh/zprof
# Once uncommenting this, reload the shell and run zprof to get the profile output
# This will profile the loading time of everything
#zmodload zsh/zprof
```

### .oh-my-zsh.pre.sh
```
# Set the theme
ZSH_THEME="powerlevel10k/powerlevel10k"

# Set the basic list of plugins to use
plugins=(
#    autoupdate
    ohmyzsh-full-autoupdate
    zsh-syntax-highlighting
    brew
    jump
    colored-man-pages
    git
    zsh-autosuggestions
    history
    vscode
    zsh-completions
    tmux
    # jenv
)

# Add mac specific plugins to use
if [[ "${OSTYPE}" == "darwin"* ]]; then
    plugins+=(macos)
fi
```

### Related Issues

_No response_

title: virtualenvwrapper plugin should not call 'deactivate' if user already deactivated
body:
### Describe the bug

The 'virtualenvwrapper' plugin's behavior of automatically deactivating the virtualenv doesn't check if the user (or some other mechanism) already deactivated. Thus it calls `deactivate` when it shouldn't, which fails and:

- Prints an error message to the console
- never unsets `$CD_VIRTUAL_ENV`, so the error keeps printing on each `chpwd`

### Steps to reproduce

1. enable `virtualenvwrapper` plugin
2. cd into a project directory that has a corresponding virtualenvwrapper environtment
3. notice the virtualenv has been activated
4. **manually** deactivate
5. cd out of the project directory
6. notice the error text on the console:
```console
> cd
workon_cwd:24: command not found: deactivate
```
7. continue to `cd` around, noticing the error text repeating

### Expected behavior

The `virtualenvwrapper` plugin should notice the virtualenv has been deactivated, and gracefully clean up its state.

macOS 12.2

body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/discussions/10690
Originally posted by **erychagov** February 13, 2022 Hello. I notice that I have slow start of ohmyzsh. I found that the next line takes about 20 secs on my PC: ``` zcompdump_revision="#omz revision: $(builtin cd -q "$ZSH"; git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null)" ``` I found this line in the `$ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh` file. Why we need it? I want to find a way to speedup it. ---- > if you cd to $ZSH and then run > ``` > time git rev-parse HEAD > ``` > how long does it take? `git rev-parse HEAD 0.01s user 0.00s system 76% cpu 0.014 total` > that looks normal. how did you determine the offending line? I'm assuming by adding echo or printf statements until you zeroed in on it? You're right! I used `echo` to find the line. ---- > Does this happen on each new terminal window? Only first terminal window takes time > Do you see the same slow behavior when running No.

title: A bug occurs when using the tab key
body:
### Describe the bug

When I use the Zsh with ohmyzsh installed under the root user, I use the tab key to complete the command. If I press the tab repeatedly, the first two or more letters of the command will appear repeatedly. For example, if I enter the clear command and press the tab key twice or three times, clclear will appear. If I press the backspace key to delete, only the five letters of ‘clear’ can be deleted, The previous ‘clea’ cannot be deleted, and pressing the Enter key cannot delete ’clea'

This situation occurs on both Pengwin and Debian of WSL, but not on other Linux distributions


### Steps to reproduce

1. Enter 'cle'
┌─[root@Shadow] - [~] - [43]
└─[$] cle

2. Press tab to complete the command
┌─[root@Shadow] - [~] - [43]
└─[$] cleaclear                                                                                        
clear          clear_console

3. Press backspace to clear the entire command
┌─[root@Shadow] - [~] - [43]
└─[$] clea                                                                             
clear          clear_console

Now the letter "clea" in front can't be cleared away

### Expected behavior

After pressing backspace normally, the CLE will also be cleared

When the tab key is normally pressed to complete the ‘cle’, the terminal will display ‘clear’ instead of ‘cleaclear’

_No response_

Pengwin on Windows 10 x86_64

5.8

### Oh My Zsh version

master (ef3f7c4)

Windows Terminal

author: ShadowXTz

title: Support the rush command
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Rush is microsoft's monorepo build tool.

It has built in support for autocompletion for bash, but not for zsh.



### Describe the proposed feature.

Add a plugin to support the rush cli.

```
# rush -h

Rush Multi-Project Build Tool 5.62.4 (unmanaged) - https://rushjs.io
Node.js version is 17.6.0 (unstable)

Your version of Node.js (17.6.0) is an odd-numbered release. These releases frequently have bugs. Please consider installing a Long Term Support (LTS) version instead.

usage: rush [-h] [-d] <command> ...

Rush makes life easier for JavaScript developers who develop, build, and
publish many packages from a central Git repo. It is designed to handle very
large repositories supporting many projects and people. Rush provides
policies, protections, and customizations that help coordinate teams and
safely onboard new contributors. Rush also generates change logs and
automates package publishing. It can manage decoupled subsets of projects
with different release and versioning strategies. A full API is included to
facilitate integration with other automation tools. If you are looking for a
proven turnkey solution for monorepo management, Rush is for you.

Positional arguments:
  <command>
    add                 Adds one or more dependencies to the package.json and
                        runs rush upgrade.
    change              Records changes made to projects, indicating how the
                        package version number should be bumped for the next
                        publish.
    check               Checks each project's package.json files and ensures
                        that all dependencies are of the same version
                        throughout the repository.
    deploy              Prepares a deployment by copying a subset of Rush
                        projects and their dependencies to a target folder
    init                Initializes a new repository to be managed by Rush
    init-autoinstaller  Initializes a new autoinstaller
    init-deploy         Creates a deployment scenario config file for use
                        with "rush deploy".
    install             Install package dependencies for all projects in the
                        repo according to the shrinkwrap file
    link                Create node_modules symlinks for all projects
    list                List package information for all projects in the repo
    publish             Reads and processes package publishing change
                        requests generated by "rush change".
    purge               For diagnostic purposes, use this command to delete
                        caches and other temporary files used by Rush
    scan                When migrating projects into a Rush repo, this
                        command is helpful for detecting undeclared
                        dependencies.
    setup               (EXPERIMENTAL) Invoke this command before working in
                        a new repo to ensure that any required prerequisites
                        are installed and permissions are configured.
    unlink              Delete node_modules symlinks for all projects in the
                        repo
    update              Install package dependencies for all projects in the
                        repo, and create or update the shrinkwrap file as
                        needed
    update-autoinstaller
                        Updates autoinstaller package dependenices
    update-cloud-credentials
                        (EXPERIMENTAL) Update the credentials used by the
                        build cache provider.
    version             Manage package versions in the repo.
    build               Build all projects that haven't been built, or have
                        changed since they were last built.
    rebuild             Clean and rebuild the entire set of projects.
    tab-complete        Provides tab completion.

Optional arguments:
  -h, --help            Show this help message and exit.
  -d, --debug           Show the full call stack if an error occurs while
                        executing the tool
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

None.  I just think this is a good idea to add to the already rich set of plugins.

### Additional context

```
# rush tab-complete

add
change
check
deploy
init
init-autoinstaller
init-deploy
install
link
list
publish
purge
scan
setup
unlink
update
update-autoinstaller
update-cloud-credentials
version
build
rebuild
--debug
-d
#
```

### Related Issues

_No response_

author: codebysandwich

title: aws plugin doesn't allow empty ZSH_THEME_AWS_PREFIX or ZSH_THEME_AWS_SUFFIX
body:
When writing a theme, if I specifically set `ZSH_THEME_AWS_SUFFIX` to an empty string, ie

```zsh
ZSH_THEME_AWS_SUFFIX=''
```

then I expect the output of `aws_prompt_info` to NOT finish with '>'

The issue appears to be caused by the use of `:=` in `aws_prompt_info()`, which is remedied by changing it to `-` (or `=`)

Here's the "fixed" `aws_prompt_info()`

```zsh
function aws_prompt_info() {
  [[ -n "$AWS_PROFILE" ]] || return
  echo "${ZSH_THEME_AWS_PREFIX-<aws:}${AWS_PROFILE:gs/%/%%}${ZSH_THEME_AWS_SUFFIX->}"
}
```

author: anandb-ripencc

title: upgrade.sh doesn't change "unauthenticated git protocol on port 9418
body:
### Describe the bug

Due to [GitHub Blog about Git protocol security](https://github.blog/2021-09-01-improving-git-protocol-security-github/), the `unencrypted Git protocol` protocol is no longer supported since March 15, 2022. If users used `git` protocol to pull Oh-My-Zsh then fatal information will be triggered when he/she tries to upgrade now:
> fatal: remote error: 
  The unauthenticated git protocol on port 9418 is no longer supported.
Please see https://github.blog/2021-09-01-improving-git-protocol-security-github/ for more information.

It's caused by the deprecated `git` protocol and `upgrade.sh` in Oh-My-Zsh won't change the protocol automatically.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Try `git clone git://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.git`, and the fatal information will show up.
2. If an old version ohmyzsh is used and the update information shows when opening a new shell, press `n` to cancel update, `cd $ZSH`, and change the remote repo url by `git remote set-url origin "git://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.git"`. Then close and reopen the shell, try to update ohmyzsh, the fatal information will show up.

### Expected behavior

ohmyzsh will automatically change protocol and update

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Ubuntu 20.04

### Zsh version

5.8

### Oh My Zsh version

master (511ed654)

### Terminal emulator

Windows Terminal

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

WSL2

### Additional context

_No response_

author: adriens

title: 1Password plugin isn't working with 1Password CLI 2
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

1Password

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

The new CLI of 1Password is breaking the `1password` plugin – it introduces a new command structure.

See Docs: [https://developer.1password.com/docs/cli/upgrade](https://developer.1password.com/docs/cli/upgrade)
> 1Password CLI 2 introduces a noun-verb command structure that groups commands by topic rather than by operation.

Therefore the list command that's currently used by the plugin isn't working anymore.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Use the "Update your scripts" table, to support 1password cli 2: [https://developer.1password.com/docs/cli/upgrade#step-2-update-your-scripts](https://developer.1password.com/docs/cli/upgrade#step-2-update-your-scripts).

### Describe alternatives you've considered

A workaround for now is not upgrading the 1Password CLI and stay with the predecessor for now. Though it would be nice to at least add a notice to the README.

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

_No response_

title: matcher-list zstyle breaks completion when multiple items have the same help message
body:
#### Reproducibility

In standard zsh, this problem doesn't exist. If I source the stock version of oh-my-zsh, the problem exists.

I ruled out any of the setopt options.

#### What doesn't work

If the completion function is this:
```
_example() {
    integer ret=1
    local -a args
    args+=(
        '--enumerable-flag-case-1[My shared help message.]'
        '--enumerable-flag-case-2[My shared help message.]'
    )
    _arguments -w -s -S $args[@] && ret=0

    return ret
}
```

The output for completing from `--` is broken:
```
--enumerable-flag-case-2                                                                                                                                                                     
--enumerable-flag-case-1                                                                                                                                                                     
-- My shared help message.                                                                                                                                                                          
--enumerable-flag-case-2                                                                                                                                                                     
--enumerable-flag-case-1                                                                                                                                                                     
-- My shared help message.                                                                                                                                                                          
--enumerable-flag-case-2                                                                                                                                                                     
--enumerable-flag-case-1                                                                                                                                                                     
-- My shared help message.                                                                                                                                                                          
```

#### What does work

But if the completion function is this:
```
_example() {
    integer ret=1
    local -a args
    args+=(
        '--enumerable-flag-case-1[My unique help message 1.]'
        '--enumerable-flag-case-2[My unique help message 2.]'
    )
    _arguments -w -s -S $args[@] && ret=0

    return ret
}
```

The output for completing from `--` is correct:
```
--enumerable-flag-case-1  -- My unique help message 1.
--enumerable-flag-case-2  -- My unique help message 2.
```

title: Weird environment/shell variables with commands handling when using Heredoc

### Describe the bug

I expected a variable substitution in a heredoc.  For example:
``` sh
> a=helloworld
> cat <<EOF
> export a=$a
> EOF

This should print _export a=helloworld_.  Instead, it prints _export a=_  There's nothing after the equal sign.  And not only that, **_a_** is also changed to be blank.  That is, after the cat command with heredoc above, the value of _a_  becomes blank.

sh-5.1$ abc=alpha

alpha

author: PierreDemailly

title: Set up ohmyzsh/wiki with sync with project wiki
body:
Based on internal information by @LunarWatcher:

> Found the script:
> ```yml
> script:
> - |
>   git config user.name "<name>"
>   git config user.email "<email>"
>   git remote -v
>   git remote remove origin
>   git remote add origin https://$GITHUB_API_KEY@github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh-wiki.git > /dev/null 2>&1
>   git remote add upstream https://$GITHUB_API_KEY@github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.wiki.git > /dev/null 2>&1
>   git fetch origin
>   git fetch upstream
>   git merge origin/master --no-edit
>   git push upstream HEAD:master > /dev/null 2>&1
> ```
>
> Obligatory note that this was travis-compatible and NOT GitHub actions, so probably not directly compatible.

It should be easy to set up the repo with the right permissions and set up the GitHub Action.

author: fiver22

title: Add git function to amend an old commit
body:
### Describe the proposed feature.
```
# git-amend-old
# Stash, apply to past commit, and rebase the current branch on to of the result.
# https://github.com/cirosantilli/dotfiles/blob/72584f5126e59b6ef6a6658210df4a8d38c78dfd/home/.bashrc#L1824
function gco!() {
  current_branch="$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)"
  apply_to="$1"
  git stash
  git checkout "$apply_to"
  git stash apply
  git add -u
  git commit --amend --no-edit
  new_sha="$(git log --format="%H" -n 1)"
  git checkout "$current_branch"
  git rebase --onto "$new_sha" "$apply_to"
}
```

title: Themes should not show ".local" in prompts
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

gnzh

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Mac hostnames end in ".local", which need not be shown at the shell prompt. I'm using gnzh now with this problem, but others may suffer with this as well.

### Describe the proposed feature.

As [discussed on Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/MacOS/comments/u7zfyd/how_to_remove_local_from_prompts_host/i5j4z3m/)

> [https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/themes/gnzh.zsh-theme#L22](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/themes/gnzh.zsh-theme#L22) looks like they're using %M rather than %m. Maybe they'd like a patch to make that optional? Some extra variable that toggles between %m and %M on lines 22/24 of that file.
> 
> Maybe just open a github issue and request the feature. It shouldn't be hard to implement. 
> 
> Even simpler than toggling it, one could just use different logic in the two sides of that "is this an ssh connection?" conditional. Arguably, for SSH connections it should be fully qualified %M (you really don't want to mistake which machine you're on) and for non-SSH connections it should be just the local part %m. Do you see what I mean? Locally, %M is overkill and makes the prompt excessively long.
> 
> [https://zsh.sourceforge.io/Doc/Release/Prompt-Expansion.html#Login-information](https://zsh.sourceforge.io/Doc/Release/Prompt-Expansion.html#Login-information):
> 
> >%M  
> The full machine hostname.  
> %m  
> The hostname up to the first ‘.’. An integer may follow the ‘%’ to specify how many components of the hostname are desired. With a negative integer, trailing components of the hostname are shown.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

author: luojinghui

title: omz plugin disable Command Tool Deletes .zshrc Symlink
body:
### Describe the bug

I have my config file in a repo and I set a symlink from `~/.zshrc` to my repo

```
ll ~/.zshrc
lrwxrwxrwx 1 fernando fernando 45 Apr 25 22:32 /home/fernando/.zshrc -> /home/fernando/workspace/config/desktop/zshrc
```

After I run `omz plugin disable PLUGIN` the symlink is removed and my `/home/fernando/workspace/config/desktop/zshrc` moved into `~/.zshrc`

After a 5 min search on the repo. It seems the issue is how `omz` handles the `disable` [action](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/30e23a643ba4f892c42e8d8d8a2ab31a6e68a9a6/lib/cli.zsh#L283) by creating a new `$zdot/.zshrc.new` file and then replacing `$zdot/.zshrc.new` with the original `$zdot/.zshrc` (`mv -f "$zdot/.zshrc.new" "$zdot/.zshrc"`)

I did not test this issue with other `omz plugins` commands. I do not know if `omz plugins enable` also fails but by looking into the code it should behave the same

### Steps to reproduce

Disable any plugin while the `zshrc` config file is a symlink 
```
❯ ls -l ~/.zshrc
lrwxrwxrwx 1 fernando fernando 45 Apr 25 22:32 /home/fernando/.zshrc -> /home/fernando/workspace/config/desktop/zshrc

❯ omz plugin disable fzf
omz::plugin::disable: plugins disabled: fzf.

❯ ls -l ~/.zshrc
-rw-rw-r-- 1 fernando fernando 5.0K Apr 25 22:38 /home/fernando/.zshrc
```

### Expected behavior

I expect the symlink to remain like:

```
❯ ls -l ~/.zshrc
lrwxrwxrwx 1 fernando fernando 45 Apr 25 22:32 /home/fernando/.zshrc -> /home/fernando/workspace/config/desktop/zshrc

❯ omz plugin disable fzf
omz::plugin::disable: plugins disabled: fzf.

❯ ls -l ~/.zshrc
lrwxrwxrwx 1 fernando fernando 45 Apr 25 22:32 /home/fernando/.zshrc -> /home/fernando/workspace/config/desktop/zshrc
```

Or get a warning/error message at least. This is silently replacing how I have my configuration with no easy way to find out

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

POP OS 20.04

### Zsh version

5.8

### Oh My Zsh version

master (eb00b95)

### Terminal emulator

alacritty

title: Add new alias for `npm install-test`
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

npm plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Once in a growing project (without CI/CD) I had a task that required me to go through some repos and assure that every test in the `main` branch were correct. At first I thought I could run `npm install` and `npm test` for each repo, but during the task I realized  that I could look for an `npm` command that do the same thing as me, and [it does exist!](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v7/commands/npm-install) So I looked for an alias in omz to be even quicker. Saddly, there is no alias for this.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Just create a alias (may `npmIt`) that runs `npm install-test`.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

In this same task I had problems with some packages that were solved using `npm install-test --force`. But I think this could be a desnecessary/forgotten alias since it solves a very specific problem.

### Additional context

 I created an alias myself in `zshrc` file, but I would like to add to the project to help anyone out there who passed the same situation as I did.

If I'm missing something, just let me know. But I already prepared a [PR](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/pull/10891) for this change.

Git plugin

Too many local branches that are gone on remote.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Add a new alias to delete all local branches which are gone on remote.
Right now I am using this:

git fetch -p && for branch in `git branch -vv | grep ': gone]' | awk '{print $1}'`; do git branch -D $branch; done

title: Website Install section contains a `$` that users will copy
body: ### Describe the bug On the public website in the install instructions there is a `$` to start the command. Now, you know, and I know, that this `$` is not supposed to be copied and entered into the terminal but that will not stop people from doing just that. I _know better_ and I just did that by not paying attention and just triple clicking to select the whole code block. as you can see in the image above that text is selectable as is. ### The Fix Thankfully the fix is easy. You can simply take the element that has the code snippet remove the text `$ ` from the code block element and that will remove it from the user selectable text on the page Then you can add it back in in a non user selectable way with CSS. This will do it ```css p.code::before { content: "$ "; } ``` That will add the `$` back as a visual cue but not make it user selectable text so that users can more easily select the text and paste what they selected directly into terminal ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open website 2. triple click code block 3. notice that the `$ ` is copied as well as the actual code snippet ### Expected behavior the `$ ` should not be selectable so that users do not paste that into their terminal and get ``` command not found: $ ``` ### Screenshots and recordings _No response_ ### OS / Linux distribution MacOS 12.3.1 (21E258) ### Zsh version latest (not actually installed yet because of the $) ### Terminal emulator warp.dev ### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_

author: max-rocket-internet

title: git_prompt_status always returns empty in theme
body:
### Describe the bug

I try to make my own theme and currently ran into a weird problem. 

My goal: To have the git branch in my prompt in a color based on the current status of the repository (modified, added, ...).
I use `$(git_prompt_status)` to get the current status and a case statement with regex to look up whats currently changed.

The problem: The case statement will never return anything different than the "default action" (`*)`) because `$(git_prompt_status)` always returns an empty string. 

Even weirder: It does not work when used in my theme file. But when using the theme and calling `my_git_color` in bash it will always show the correct output (the correct color string)!

Here's the full theme:
```bash
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_ADDED="A"
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_MODIFIED="M"
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_DELETED="M"
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_RENAMED="M"
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_UNMERGED=""
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_UNTRACKED="U"

function my_git_color() {
    local state="$(git_prompt_status)"
    case "$state" in
        *"A"*) echo -n "%B%F{green}";;
        *"M"*) echo -n "%B%F{yellow}";;
        *"U"*) echo -n "%F{141}";;
        *) echo -n "%F{red}";;
    esac
}

ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_PREFIX="%F{blue} [$(my_git_color)"
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_SUFFIX="%b%F{blue}]%f "
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_DIRTY=""
ZSH_THEME_GIT_PROMPT_CLEAN=""
ZSH_THEME_VIRTUALENV_PREFIX="%F{white}[%B%F{green}"
ZSH_THEME_VIRTUALENV_SUFFIX="%b%F{white}]%f "

PROMPT_CHAR="%B%F{grey}\$%b%f"
PROMPT='%B%(?:%F{green}→:%F{red}→)%b%f %F{cyan}%c%f $PROMPT_CHAR%f '
RPROMPT='$(virtualenv_prompt_info)$(git_prompt_info)'
```
You can safely ignore the virtualenv lines. The error already was there before I added them.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use the theme above
2. `cd` into a local git repository with changes
3. See that the color is always red, no matter what you do
4. Call my_git_color to see it _should_ work

### Expected behavior

The git branch should have different colors based on the current changes in the repository

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Windows 11, Ubuntu WSL

### Zsh version

5.8

### Oh My Zsh version

master (8f56a8bd)

### Terminal emulator

Windows Terminal Preview

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

WSL2

### Additional context

_No response_

title: Compile a meaningful list of features and changes that OMZ does to ZSH
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

There is no feature list.

When going through a README or FAQ, it has no information about what the OMZ actually does to ZSH, besides plugins, themes, and some vague marketing clickbait cthing.

When I typed `alias` at some point, I was hell of a lot surprised to see a bunch of aliases that I never defined appear out of nowhere. Took me a while to realise it's courtesy of omz.

After I copy-pasted a bunch of text, which was starting with "title", into a wrong terminal window, I was pretty surprised to find out that there is a `title` function exists now. And the surprise wasn't pretty at all.


### Describe the proposed feature.

These are the things that people should be able to find out about by reading the documentation, not by reading the source code, as someone suggested in the Discord. And definitely not by an accident.

Would be nice to have things like that be documented somewhere.

There's plenty of documentation parsers/generators out there, but this project doesn't seem to be changing _that_ actively now, so vast majority of functions, aliases, shortcuts, and changes, that are built-in (not provided by plugins) can be safely documented.

After all, plugins do have a meaningful readme, why is the core doesn't?


### Describe alternatives you've considered

I've tried going though the source code, but that is like asking someone to learn C++ by going through the compiler source code.

author: josenafria

title: Add node_modules/.bin to path
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

When working with Node.js, it can be useful to run commands from the local `node_modules` folder. So for example instead of `yarn eslint .` or `npx eslint .`, one could simply run `eslint .`

### Describe the proposed feature.

Add `node_modules/.bin` to path. For monorepo support, this should be done for each `node_modules/.bin` folder found up the parent tree of the current working directory. It should also be cleaned up.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

I have been using this feature for a while now and I like it. I think it might be useful for others too.

```zsh
function precmd() {
  # Add node_modules/.bin to path
  path=( ${path[@]:#*node_modules*} )
  local p="$(pwd)"
  while [[ "$p" != '/' ]]; do
    if [[ -d "$p/node_modules/.bin" ]]; then
      path+=("$p/node_modules/.bin")
    fi
    p="$(dirname "$p")"
  done
  typeset -U path
}
```

It’s important the local paths are appended, not prepended, so these commands don’t override existing commands. I.e. a malicious package could register the binary `ls`.

This works with both npm and yarn, and any other package managers that store executables in `node_modules/.bin`.

### Related Issues

_No response_
author: remcohaszing

title: `clipcopy` with `wl-clipboard` and `xclip` does not free a TTY
body:
### Describe the bug

It is known fact that when copying with `wl-copy` in Wayland or `xclip` in X, the both tools go to the background to keep clipboard data owned by themselves, but do not detach from a TTY, so terminals do not close automatically even after you quit a shell.

The reason for OMZ `clipcopy` to suffer from it is [here for `wl-clipboard`](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/8f56a8bdf39d7727ab0e220f0164f78c77f9c50e/lib/clipboard.zsh#L61) and [here for `xclip`](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/8f56a8bdf39d7727ab0e220f0164f78c77f9c50e/lib/clipboard.zsh#L64). In my opinion, such a poor behavior is not an Oh My Zsh bug, but `xclip` and `wl-copy` do this for ages, so it looks better to workaround it.

In opposite, `xsel` detaches from a TTY completely after copying.

### Steps to reproduce

On any modern Linux distribution with `wl-clipboard` and `xclip` installed try:

To reproduce with `xclip`
1. Boot into X session or `unset WAYLAND_DISPLAY`
2. `echo 123 | clipcopy`
3. Quit shell
4. Terminal will keep running until you copy something else or kill `xclip` or close it explicitly

To reproduce with `wl-clipboard`
1. Boot into Wayland session or `unset DISPLAY`
2. `echo 123 | clipcopy`
3. Quit shell
4. Terminal will keep running until you copy something else or kill `wl-copy` or close it explicitly


### Expected behavior

`clipcopy` should detach from TTY

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Arch Linux 

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

master

### Terminal emulator

kitty

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

_No response_

### Additional context

Suggested changes are in PR #10953.

For Wayland, change:

    wl-copy < "${1:-/dev/stdin}"

to:

    cat "${1:-/dev/stdin}" | nohup wl-copy &>/dev/null

or may be even bettter to [Zsh-specific disowning launch](https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/243315/in-zsh-whats-the-difference-between-and/243316#243316):

    cat "${1:-/dev/stdin}" | { wl-copy &>/dev/null &| }

And the respectively for X with `xclip`:

    xclip -in -selection clipboard < "${1:-/dev/stdin}"

to:

    cat "${1:-/dev/stdin}" | { xclip -selection clipboard -in &>/dev/null &| }

title: MacOS bluetooth restart command (btrestart) does not work
body:
### Describe the bug

MacOS: Monterey 12.2.1
Platform: M1 Pro

When running the `btrestart` command I receive the following error(s)

`Executing: /usr/bin/kmutil unload -b com.apple.iokit.BroadcomBluetoothHostControllerUSBTransport
Error Domain=KMErrorDomain Code=71 "Kernel request failed: (libkern/kext) kext is in use or retained (cannot unload) (-603946984)" UserInfo={NSLocalizedDescription=Kernel request failed: (libkern/kext) kext is in use or retained (cannot unload) (-603946984)}
Executing: /usr/bin/kmutil load -b com.apple.iokit.BroadcomBluetoothHostControllerUSBTransport`


### Steps to reproduce

1. Open terminal/iterm2 on a new Mac running Monterey
2. type `btrestart`
3. hit enter. 


### Expected behavior

BT services should restart. 

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS Monterey 12.2.1

### Zsh version

5.8

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

_No response_

### Additional context

After doing some digging it does seem on newer versions of macOS the specific restart commands used do not fully work anylonger. 

[The link attached](https://gist.github.com/nicolasembleton/afc19940da26716f8e90?permalink_comment_id=3879109#gistcomment-3879109) shows that, as well as a new suggestion which does work for me reliably when written into a custom bash script and run. 

(specifically:) 

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

pgrep audio | xargs sudo kill
pgrep bluetooth | xargs sudo kill

sudo launchctl list | grep -i blue | awk '{ print $3 }' | xargs sudo launchctl stop
sudo launchctl list | grep -i blue | awk '{ print $3 }' | xargs sudo launchctl start
sudo launchctl list | grep -i audio | awk '{ print $3 }' | xargs sudo launchctl stop
sudo launchctl list | grep -i audio | awk '{ print $3 }' | xargs sudo launchctl start
```

Would like to see that, or a more namespace safe version rolled into the omz macOS plugin.
author: mikeohara

title: zsh throws some script after log in and all functionality is broken then
body:
### Describe the bug

Hello all,
zsh runs something like a script when logged in and all of functionality is broken. I have installed the following:

- zsh 5.9-1
- zsh-autosuggestions 0.7.0-1
- zsh-syntax-highlighting 0.7.1-1
- zsh-theme-powerlevel110k 1.16.1-1

zshrc is:
```
# Lines configured by zsh-newuser-install
HISTFILE=~/.histfile
HISTSIZE=1000
SAVEHIST=1000
bindkey -e
# End of lines configured by zsh-newuser-install
# The following lines were added by compinstall
zstyle :compinstall filename '/home/observer/.zshrc'

autoload -Uz compinit
compinit
# End of lines added by compinstall
```
no oh-my-zsh is installed (I manually deleted ~.oh-my-zsh directory)

and each login it throws [this script](https://pastebin.pl/view/57ec7688)

I have found [the only link in google](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/issues/5710) but solution there doesnt help.

What happened? Why doesnt it work?

Thanks in advance.



### Steps to reproduce

just log in and zsh throws a script

### Expected behavior

```
compdump () {
        # undefined
        builtin autoload -XUz
}
compinit () {

        setopt extendedglob
        typeset _i_dumpfile _i_files _i_line _i_done _i_dir _i_autodump=1
        typeset _i_tag _i_file _i_addfiles _i_fail=ask _i_check=yes _i_name
        while [[ $# -gt 0 && $1 = -[dDiuC] ]]

                                then

                                        shift
                                fi ;;
                        (-D) _i_autodump=0
                                shift ;;
                        (-i) _i_fail=ign
                                shift ;;
                        (-u) _i_fail=use
                                shift ;;
                        (-C) _i_check=
                                shift ;;
                esac
        done
        typeset -gHA _comps _services _patcomps _postpatcomps
        typeset -gHA _compautos
        typeset -gHA _lastcomp
        if [[ -n $_i_dumpfile ]]
        then
                typeset -g _comp_dumpfile="$_i_dumpfile"
        else
                typeset -g _comp_dumpfile="${ZDOTDIR:-$HOME}/.zcompdump"
        fi
        typeset -gHa _comp_options
        _comp_options=(bareglobqual extendedglob glob multibyte multifuncdef nullglob rcexpandparam unset NO_allexport NO_aliases NO_cshnullglob NO_cshjunkiequotes NO_errexit NO_errreturn NO_globassign NO_globsubst NO_histsubstpattern NO_ignorebraces NO_ignoreclosebraces NO_kshglob NO_ksharrays NO_kshtypeset NO_markdirs NO_octalzeroes NO_posixbuiltins NO_posixidentifiers NO_shwordsplit NO_shglob NO_warnnestedvar NO_warncreateglobal)
        typeset -gH _comp_setup='local -A _comp_caller_options;
             _comp_caller_options=(${(kv)options[@]});
             setopt localoptions localtraps localpatterns ${_comp_options[@]};
             local IFS=$'\'\ \\t\\r\\n\\0\'';
             builtin enable -p \| \~ \( \? \* \[ \< \^ \# 2>&-;
             exec </dev/null;
             trap - ZERR;
             local -a reply;
             local REPLY;
             local REPORTTIME;
             unset REPORTTIME'
        typeset -ga compprefuncs comppostfuncs
        compprefuncs=()
        comppostfuncs=()
        : $funcstack
        compdef () {
                local opt autol type func delete eval new i ret=0 cmd svc
                local -a match mbegin mend
                emulate -L zsh
                setopt extendedglob
                if (( ! $# ))
                then
                        print -u2 "$0: I need arguments"
                        return 1
                fi
                while getopts "anpPkKde" opt
                do
                        case "$opt" in
                                (a) autol=yes  ;;
                                (n) new=yes  ;;
                                ([pPkK]) if [[ -n "$type" ]]
                                        then
                                                print -u2 "$0: type already set to $type"
                                                return 1
                                        fi
                                        if [[ "$opt" = p ]]
                                        then
                                                type=pattern
                                        elif [[ "$opt" = P ]]

                                        elif [[ "$opt" = K ]]
                                        then
                                                type=widgetkey
                                        else
                                                type=key
                                        fi ;;
                                (d) delete=yes  ;;

                done
                shift OPTIND-1
                if (( ! $# ))
                then
                        print -u2 "$0: I need arguments"

                fi

                                done
                                return ret
                        fi
                        func="$1"
                        [[ -n "$autol" ]] && autoload -Uz "$func"
                        shift
                        case "$type" in
                                (widgetkey) while [[ -n $1 ]]
                                        do
                                                if [[ $# -lt 3 ]]

                                                        return 1
                                                fi
                                                [[ $1 = _* ]] || 1="_$1"
                                                [[ $2 = .* ]] || 2=".$2"
                                                [[ $2 = .menu-select ]] && zmodload -i zsh/complist
                                                zle -C "$1" "$2" "$func"

                                        fi
                                        if [[ $1 = .* ]]

                                                                        then
                                                                                _postpatcomps[$match[1]]="=$match[2]=$func"
                                                                        else
                                                                                _postpatcomps[$1]="$func"
                                                                        fi ;;
                                                                (*) if [[ "$1" = *\=* ]]
                                                                        then
                                                                                cmd="${1%%\=*}"
                                                                                svc=yes
                                                                        else
                                                                                cmd="$1"
                                                                                svc=
                                                                        fi
                                                                        if [[ -z "$new" || -z "${_comps[$1]}" ]]
                                                                        then
                                                                                _comps[$cmd]="$func"
                                                                                [[ -n "$svc" ]] && _services[$cmd]="${1#*\=}"
                                                                        fi ;;
                                                        esac
                                                fi
                                                shift
                                        done ;;
                        esac
                else
                        case "$type" in
                                (pattern) unset "_patcomps[$^@]" ;;
                                (postpattern) unset "_postpatcomps[$^@]" ;;
                                (key) print -u2 "$0: cannot restore key bindings"
                                        return 1 ;;
                                (*) unset "_comps[$^@]" ;;
                        esac
                fi
        }
        typeset _i_wdirs _i_wfiles
        _i_wdirs=()
        _i_wfiles=()
        autoload -Uz compaudit
        if [[ -n "$_i_check" ]]
        then
                typeset _i_q
                if ! eval compaudit
                then
                        if [[ -n "$_i_q" ]]
                        then
                                if [[ "$_i_fail" = ask ]]
                                then

Ignore insecure $_i_q and continue [y] or abort compinit [n]? "

        if [[ ${_i_line[2]} = expand-or-complete ]] && zstyle -a ':completion:' completer _i_line && (( ${_i_line[(i)_expand]} <= ${#_i_line} ))
        then

        fi
        unfunction compinit compaudit

        return 0
}
compinstall () {
        # undefined
        builtin autoload -XUz
}

author: akashdiphazra

title: [nvm] NVM_LAZY & `nvm use <non-default-version>` does not switch node version
body:
### Describe the bug

I have enabled `NVM_LAZY`, and when I execute `nvm use <non-default-version>` (eg: if default is v16, and try `nvm use v18`), it does not switch the node version to 18

After analysis of `nvm use <version>`, I figured that it executes `$(node --version)` followed by `$(npm --version)`. Since these are subshells, the [`unfunction`](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/plugins/nvm/nvm.plugin.zsh#L34) call - only undefines the [lazy loading wrapper function](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/plugins/nvm/nvm.plugin.zsh#L33-L37) in its specific subshell. It does not unfunction in parent context & in other subshell. This means that [`nvm use default`](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/plugins/nvm/nvm.plugin.zsh#L35) statement is executed everytime even though a different version (*v18*) is provided.



### Steps to reproduce

1. `export NVM_LAZY=1` in .zshrc
2. Enable plugin `nvm`
3. Pre-requistes: Launch terminal, and execute
  
    ```
    nvm install --lts=gallium
    nvm install v18
    # In my case the default is set to gallium
    ```
   close terminal
5. Launch terminal, and execute `nvm use v18`


### Expected behavior

The node version should be v18.x.x (In my case v18.2.0)

### Screenshots and recordings

```
❯ nvm ls
       v16.15.0
        v18.2.0
         system
default -> lts/gallium (-> v16.15.0)
iojs -> N/A (default)
unstable -> N/A (default)
node -> stable (-> v18.2.0) (default)
stable -> 18.2 (-> v18.2.0) (default)
lts/* -> lts/gallium (-> v16.15.0)
lts/argon -> v4.9.1 (-> N/A)
lts/boron -> v6.17.1 (-> N/A)
lts/carbon -> v8.17.0 (-> N/A)
lts/dubnium -> v10.24.1 (-> N/A)
lts/erbium -> v12.22.12 (-> N/A)
lts/fermium -> v14.19.3 (-> N/A)
lts/gallium -> v16.15.0
❯ nvm version
none
❯ nvm use v18
Now using node v18.2.0 (npm vNow using node v16.15.0 (npm v8.10.0)
8.10.0)
❯ nvm version
Now using node v16.15.0 (npm v8.10.0)
v16.15.0
```

### OS / Linux distribution

Ubuntu 22.04

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Terminal emulator

Alacritty

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: zsh completion wrapper for Git fails in case KSH_ZERO_SUBSCRIPT is set
body:
### Describe the bug

Hi @felipec (assuming, you are still the maintainer for the zsh Git completion wrapper), 
First of all: thank you for the bash-completion wrapper, but, I am afraid, there appears to be an issue with its subsequent argument indexing and counting, when `KSH_ZERO_SUBSCRIPT` is set. 

As the completion script lacks comments, I wasn't able to figure it out exactly,  but it appears you are trying to adjust the indexing between zsh (base one) and bash (base zero) when passing arguments. Would it be possible to take the setting into account before adjusting an argument's index?

Thank you.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Unset `KSH_ZERO_SUBSCRIPT` and try `git checkout ma<TAB>` (should complete the branch's name to (at least) the `main` or `master` branch of a repository)
2. Set `KSH_ZERO_SUBSCRIPT` and try `git checkout ma<TAB>` (completion fails)

### Expected behavior

Completion should be successful, regardless of `KSH_ZERO_SUBSCRIPT`.

### Steps to reproduce

title: Remove extra space inserted when typing asterisk
body: ### Describe the bug Oh My Zsh automatically adds extra space when typing asterisk after selecting a file with the **tab** key. ### Steps to reproduce Here is the scenario. Let's say I am in a directory ("Downloads") Now I type `rm -rf` and press the **tab** key for the list of files to show up. So I get: I press **tab** again (three times) and select `file1` Notice that the cursor is just after last character of `file1`. Now I type `*` after it. What I get is this: oh-my-zsh automatically inserts an extra space between `file1` and `*`. This is disastrous because it will delete all files and folders in this directory. I have lost a bunch of files multiple times because of this behavior of oh-my-zsh. This does not exist in the default zsh shell. How can I fix this problem? ### Expected behavior The default zsh behavior does not add the extra space after hitting the tab key. author: ricpelo

title: `emacs -nw` does not work with `emacs` plugin
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/discussions/10936
Originally posted by **iago-pssjd** May 17, 2022 I am trying to open emacs in my zsh terminal with `emacs -nw` but it does not open emacs, at least visibly in the terminal (the command works in bash terminal). What does it happen? My OS is Debian 11, my default terminal `zsh`, and my zsh-theme is `powerlevel9k`. If I can give any other clue about my system, let me know. Thanks!
The default `emacs` alias adds the `--no-wait` flag. When `emacs -nw` is used instead of the `te` alias, the two conflict and no terminal frame is opened. A solution is to remove the `--no-wait` flag if `-nw` is passed.
author: mcornella

title: common-aliases causes pygmentize error when _normal function invoked
body:
### Describe the bug

This bug was originally reported against fzf-tab-completion ( https://github.com/lincheney/fzf-tab-completion/issues/39 ) but upon investigating the bug it appears to instead be in Oh My Zsh, specifically the common-aliases plugin. Specifically, fzf-tab-completion produces the following repeated error:

```
usage: pygmentize [-l LEXER | -g] [-F FILTER[:options]] [-f FORMATTER]
                  [-O OPTION=value[,OPTION=value,...]] [-P OPTION=value]
                  [-o OUTPUTFILE] [-v] [-s] [-x]
                  [-S STYLE -f formatter | -L [WHAT ...] | -N FILENAME | -C |
                  -H NAME TYPE | -V | -h] [-a ARG]
                  [INPUTFILE]
pygmentize: error: unrecognized arguments: s
usage: pygmentize [-l LEXER | -g] [-F FILTER[:options]] [-f FORMATTER]
                  [-O OPTION=value[,OPTION=value,...]] [-P OPTION=value]
                  [-o OUTPUTFILE] [-v] [-s] [-x]
                  [-S STYLE -f formatter | -L [WHAT ...] | -N FILENAME | -C |
                  -H NAME TYPE | -V | -h] [-a ARG]
                  [INPUTFILE]
pygmentize: error: unrecognized arguments: s
usage: pygmentize [-l LEXER | -g] [-F FILTER[:options]] [-f FORMATTER]
                  [-O OPTION=value[,OPTION=value,...]] [-P OPTION=value]
                  [-o OUTPUTFILE] [-v] [-s] [-x]
                  [-S STYLE -f formatter | -L [WHAT ...] | -N FILENAME | -C |
                  -H NAME TYPE | -V | -h] [-a ARG]
                  [INPUTFILE]
pygmentize: error: unrecognized arguments: s
```

This appears, however, to be only a consequence of a problem with common-aliases. Specifically (and as per my comment on the above-linked fzf-tab-completion issue), common-aliases contains this:

```
alias -g P="2>&1| pygmentize -l pytb"
```

which converts the as-per-the-zsh-source definition of _normal:

```
 _normal () {
     local _comp_command1 _comp_command2 _comp_command precommand
     local -A opts
     zparseopts -A opts -D - P p+:-=precommand s
     (( $+opts[-s] )) || _compskip=
     (( $+opts[-P] )) && precommands=()
     (( $#precommand )) && precommands+=(${precommand#-p})
```

and so forth, to instead open:

```
 _normal () {
     local _comp_command1 _comp_command2 _comp_command precommand
     local -A opts
     zparseopts -A opts -D - 2>&1| pygmentize -l pytb p+:-=precommand s
     (( $+opts[-s] )) || _compskip=
     (( $+opts[-P] )) && precommands=()
     (( $#precommand )) && precommands+=(${precommand#-p})
```

which produces the above error when _normal is invoked by a completion function.

I have worked around this temporarily by commenting out the alias in the common-aliases module and confirmed that _normal returns to the ZSH source version and the error no longer occurs.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable common-aliases plugin
2. Invoke `functions` command to confirm above change in _normal function
3. Install fzf-tab-completion and prerequisites per instructions here: https://github.com/lincheney/fzf-tab-completion
4. Attempt to autocomplete a directory.
5. 

### Expected behavior

The directory should autocomplete without error.


### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Debian bookworm/sid under WSL

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (e9e8c6b5)

### Terminal emulator

Windows Terminal 1.14.1452.0

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

WSL2

### Additional context

_No response_

author: dheerajchand

title: emacsclient.sh makes assumptions on emacs output that don't hold
body:
### Describe the bug Using the `emacs` plugin, which adds an `e` alias, which calls `oh-my-zsh/plugins/emacs/emacsclient.sh`. Logic in that script: You call it with a file to be opened in emacs. 1. use the system's `emacsclient` to execute a bit of list that lists all active frames (i.e., what you'd call "window" in a modern GUI) 2. if there's empty string or `nil` returned, create a new frame, so show a new (X11/windows/…) window
if there's none, display in current frame Sadly, the check fails: if you add a few debug `echos`, you'll get something funky happening: ```shell # Only create another X frame if there isn't one present if [ -z "${frames}" -o "${frames}" = 'nil' ]; then echo "frames ($frames) evaluated empty or nil" emacsclient --alternate-editor "" --create-frame "$@" return $? fi echo "frames ($frames) evaluated non-nil, no new frame" emacsclient --alternate-editor "" "$@" ``` which then happily proceeds to tell us, at least when run without an emacs daemon already running, ```text frames (nil) evaluated non-nil, no new frame ``` Well, I was flabbergasted, `nil != nil`? And on the second run, suddenly `nil = nil`! Turns out, when you hexdump the non-nil `nil`, i.e., the one that emacs spits out on the second (and subsequent) run, there's a bunch of invisible characters being printed. ```text 00000000: 1b5d 3132 3b23 6666 6666 6666 6666 3030 .]12;#ffffffff00 00000010: 3030 076e 696c 0a 00.nil. ``` So, this is an unlucky combination of emacs (with plugins, but who uses vanilla emacs?) in modern versions starting some kind of TUI without much ado, which already prints something, only to be then *followed* by something printable, namely the contents we instructed to get via lisp. ### Steps to reproduce 1. enable plugin `emacs` 2. (make sure no emacs daemon is running.) Run `e ~/.zshrc` 3. Instead of starting emacs, opening a new GUI window and opening .zshrc in that, you get a quickly popping up terminal-mode emacs (which you did not request), which instantly closes again ### Expected behavior 1. enable plugin `emacs` 2. (make sure no emacs daemon is running.) Run `e ~/.zshrc` 3. opens a new GUI window and .zshrc in that ### Screenshots and recordings _No response_ ### OS / Linux distribution Fedora 36 Linux, ### Zsh version 5.8.1 ### Oh My Zsh version master (50550c70) ### Terminal emulator alacritty ### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL _No response_ ### Additional context A solution could either be 1. fix the lisp command passed to emacsclient to list frames: `(delete 't (mapcar 'framep (frame-list)))` to instead pipe the result into a (temporary) file. Read that file instead of stdout. 2. Strip all characters up to and including the 0x07 byte (ASCII Bell symbol) in the `$frames` string If I knew enough lisp to make this not only work, but work *reliable* across platforms, I'd go for 1. Alas, I don't. So, `frames="$(emacsclient -a '' -n -e "$cmd" 2>/dev/null |sed 's/.*\x07//g' )"` it is for me for now, but it's a dirty hotfix.

author: mathisve

title: Regression in tab filename completion behaviour with oh-my-zsh and zsh 5.9
body:
### Describe the bug

Good day.

Tab completion of filenames fails under certain circumstances outlined in the *steps to reproduce* section using a fresh install of oh-my-zsh and zsh 5.9. The problem appears to be limited to filenames that have a lexicographic distance of one letter of the alphabet in their first difference, and have two or more additional subsequent letters thereafter, however I have not exhaustively tested it. For instance:

- `test-file.aaa` and `test-file.bar` **will** induce the bug.
- `test-file.a` and `test.file.b` **will not**.
- `test-file.aa` and `test.file.ba` **will not**.
- `test-file.service` and `test-file.timer` **will** induce the bug.
- `test-file.aervice` and `test-file.timer` **will not**.

- **This behaviour does not occur with zsh 5.8**, as ascertained by a downgrade of the relevant package.
- I have observed this bug on two hosts with different zshrc/oh-my-zsh configurations, both running zsh 5.9.
- However the bug is not observed when I copy my everyday oh-my-zsh configuration to an Ubuntu host running zsh 5.8.

Thank you for your time.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install zsh 5.9.
2. Install oh-my-zsh via curl of GitHub repository.
3. `touch` two files called `test-file.aaa` and `test-file.bar`
4. Attempt to tab complete the string `./test`
5. Observe that the autocomplete result is `./test-file.Ba`
6. Press tab again.
7. Observe that the autocomplete result is `./test-file.bar`

### Expected behavior

Tab completion should only complete to the first difference between filenames. Successive tabs should display and cycle through the options.

### Screenshots and recordings

Anomalous behaviour after tabbing `./test` in a directory containing `test.aaa` and `test.bar`:
![gh - zsh anomalous](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62781197/174000297-cb265af1-127b-4770-9ccd-600586ac1b0d.png)

Expected behaviour after tabbing `./test` twice in a directory containing `test.a` and `test.b`:
![gh - zsh expected](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62781197/174000494-6377a08f-47d1-40aa-b381-e72ba32c9f8b.png)



### OS / Linux distribution

Manjaro Linux x86_64

### Zsh version

5.9

### Terminal emulator

Tilix

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: Installing Oh My Zsh overwrites the .zshrc instead of appending to it
body:
### Describe the bug

I use the `.zshrc` a lot, by adding my custom aliases and functions to it. Moreover, it contains some default appends from other software installations, like `nvm`.

When I installed `ohmyzsh`, it overrode the`.zshrc`. I'm glad that my `.zshrc` file is always open in an editor (I also keep a backup of it, THANK GOD), I just hit undo to get my stuff back.

In the ideal case, `ohmyzsh` should only append to the `.zshrc`, not override it. My `.zshrc` also contained few lines for `nvm` to work, which `nvm` adds on installation by default.

title: nvm plugin awfully slow
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

nvm plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Loading nvm plugin is awfully slow.  I set NVM_LAZY=1 but no difference.

My set up OSX Monteray and nvm 0.39.1 installed via brew 



### Describe the proposed feature.

solution for me was to disable it and go with a custom script found on:

https://www.reddit.com/r/node/comments/4tg5jg/lazy_load_nvm_for_faster_shell_start/d5ib9fs/?utm_term=28631259784&context=3&utm_medium=comment_embed&utm_source=embed&utm_name=null

```
declare -a NODE_GLOBALS=(`find ~/.nvm/versions/node -maxdepth 3 -type l -wholename '*/bin/*' | xargs -n1 basename | sort | uniq`)

NODE_GLOBALS+=("node")
NODE_GLOBALS+=("nvm")

load_nvm () {
    export NVM_DIR=~/.nvm
    [ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
}

for cmd in "${NODE_GLOBALS[@]}"; do
    eval "${cmd}(){ unset -f ${NODE_GLOBALS}; load_nvm; ${cmd} \$@ }"
done
```

maybe you could add that snippet behind an other variable that loads it and discards the rest
