title: Check for upgrade exits prematurely, thus auto-updates do not work
body:
### Describe the bug

When I started my shell, I noticed that the auto-updates no longer appeared to run. After debugging, I noticed that the script exits somewhere inside the below block of code. I have curl installed and expect this should run just fine, but it always fails. Unfortunately, I'm not savvy enough in shell to figure out where exactly this is failing.

This behavior is happening on both my work and personal machines. 

```
local remote_head
  remote_head=$(
    if (( ${+commands[curl]} )); then
      curl --conect-timeout 2 -fsSL -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github.v3.sha' $api_url 2>/dev/null
    elif (( ${+commands[wget]} )); then
      wget -T 2 -O- --header='Accept: application/vnd.github.v3.sha' $api_url 2>/dev/null
    elif (( ${+commands[fetch]} )); then
      HTTP_ACCEPT='Accept: application/vnd.github.v3.sha' fetch -T 2 -o - $api_url 2>/dev/null
    else
      exit 0
    fi
  ) || return 1

// I never reach here

[[ "$local_head" != "$remote_head" ]] || return 1
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Put a debug statement (echo or whatever) above the line `[[ "$local_head" != "$remote_head" ]] || return 1` in check_for_upgrade.sh
2. Open a new terminal

Notice that the debug statement is not printed

### Expected behavior

It is expected that every time the shell starts that the update scripts runs and checks to see if there are any updates.

title: Change the OMZ layout
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

OMZ dirties up my home directory

### Describe the proposed feature.

The OMZ repo is moved to ~/.config/zsh, in the home directory there is a .zshenv file:

```zsh
export ZDOTDIR=~/.config/zsh
```

The user can move the folder and change the location.

This way, the home directory remains clean. The .zshrc file can also be reduced by 90% (It can just execute all the .zsh files in $ZDOTDIR/custom):

```zsh
# .config/zsh/.zshrc
#!/usr/bin/env zsh

#!/usr/bin/env zsh

for i in $ZDOTDIR/custom/*; do
    if [[ -x "$i" ]]
    then
        . "$i"
    fi
done
```

Then there is a $ZDOTDIR/state/zsh_history file with zsh history which is ignored by git, etc, etc...

author: MenacingPerson

title: ssh-agent plugin cache mechanism should use XDG_RUNTIME_DIR
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

ssh-agent plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Currently, the mechanism for caching SSH agents just uses a loose file under `~/.ssh`, which is a bit annoying since it's usually a relatively static config directory.

### Describe the proposed feature.

This [line](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/249c708ed3a4a7a63d16a6e911a46b6fb9623cbd/plugins/ssh-agent/ssh-agent.plugin.zsh#L2) should probably use `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`. Since the state of a running SSH agent is inherently local to the current machine and to the current boot, that is in my opinion the most fitting location for such information.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Symlinking the files used to `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` manually. Very hacky and needs to be done manually for each machine used.

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

_No response_
author: FallenWarrior2k

title: ssh-agent plugin should honor existing SSH agent
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

ssh-agent plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

If an SSH agent is already configured, it is rarely desirable to start another agent. A common scenario for this is using GNOME Keyring's support for the SSH agent, in which case there will already be a running agent instance that is implicitly shared across all processes that will ever be started in the session, which also means the plugin's cache mechanism isn't needed.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Don't start a new agent instance if there is already one running, i.e. `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is set and valid.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Currently, I set `zstyle :omz:plugins:ssh-agent agent-forwarding yes`, which coincidentally also works by checking `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` before starting a new agent. However, I feel like this is abusing a control knob originally intended for something else.

### Additional context

title: Tab completion slow when autocompleting zypper command
body:
### Describe the bug

When using tab completion to autocomplete `zypper cmd`(the package manager of openSUSE), it takes some time to finish, especially when trying to complete non-existent command. This problem didn't happen in zsh without ohmyzsh, but happened after ohmyzsh being installed.

### Steps to reproduce

This problem happened on my NB and VM, but it can be easily reproduced in docker:

1. Get the docker image of openSUSE Leap 15.4 (`docker pull opensuse/leap`)
2. Run this image (`docker run --name=tmp -it opensuse/leap`)
3. Install zsh (`zypper in zsh`)
4. Steps to reproduce expected behavior:
    1. Enter `zsh` 
    2. Type `zypper sea` then press TAB, the autocompletion should work
    3. Type `zypper sar`
    4. Press TAB **several times**, then type something to check if autocompletion finished
    5. Autocompletion **finished immediately as expected**
6. Install dependencies of ohmyzsh (`zypper in curl git`)
7. Install ohmyzsh (`sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"`)
8. Steps to reproduce the problem:
    1. Type `zypper sea` then press TAB, the autocompletion should work, but **a little bit slow**
    2. Type `zypper sar`
    3. Press TAB **several times**, then type something to check if autocompletion finished
    4. Autocompletion **takes long time to finish** (about 10 secs after 30 times TAB pressed)

### Expected behavior

As described in step 4 of "Steps to reproduce" section.

title: cp, ls, wc inconsistencies
body:
### Describe the bug

Don't know whether the issue is in zsh or in Oh My Zsh.  All works in bash.
Details in https://apple.stackexchange.com/questions/444945/ghost-files-now-you-see-em-now-you-dont
Short version:
HOW is it possible in zsh+"oh-my-zsh" for cp to say the source doesn't exist, yet wc is able to read the destination in a path Finder says isn't there? And then for ls to agree that the destination doesn't exist?



### Steps to reproduce

Here's the script that failed, but you may not be able to reproduce my environment (external drive with corruption).
```
cat temp/3TB-file-list | while read LINE; do
echo $LINE
mkdir -p "~/3TB-files/$(dirname $LINE)"
cp "/Volumes/Seagate 3TB/$LINE" "~/3TB-files/$LINE"
wc "~/3TB-files/$LINE"
sleep 2
done
```

### Expected behavior

1. `mkdir -p `should create the path it is given.
2. files that can be opened by bash or by double-clicking should be copied by that script.  Finder and `ls` should see them.
3. Finder and `ls` should not say a file doesn't exist when `wc` has already shown its size.
4. script shown should behave the same in bash or zsh.

title: option to have git completion show only local branches as shown by git branch -v
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

git plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

The problem I'm running into is git completion for `git checkout` and maybe other commands shows too many completions.

Example: `git branch -v` shows just 4 branches

```
> git branch -v
  dangling-pointer       ce141cd55 Metal: Fix dangling pointer in IntermRebuild
* fix-occlusion-query-m1 67a147feb Metal: Fix Occlusion Query bug on M1
  main                   93c0c90db Suppress the failing egl test to unblock roller
  readpixel-speed-test   3ebef5614 Metal:Read Pixels Speed Test
```

But `git checkout <tab>` shows

```
❯ git checkout <tab>
zsh: do you wish to see all 6036 possibilities (776 lines)? 
```

Even `git checkout --no-guess <tab>` shows

```
> git checkout --no-guess <tab>
zsh: do you wish to see all 3298 possibilities (1113 lines)? 
```

which is 3294 more than is useful to me. `export GIT_COMPLETION_CHECKOUT_NO_GUESS=1` seems to do nothing

```
❯ export GIT_COMPLETION_CHECKOUT_NO_GUESS=1
❯ git checkout <tab>
zsh: do you wish to see all 6036 possibilities (776 lines)? 
```

### Describe the proposed feature.

I'm guessing it would be a tiny changes to add something like `GIT_COMPLETION_CHECKOUT_LOCAL_ONLY` had have it filter the same way `git branch` filters



### Describe alternatives you've considered

Not really sure what else to consider. Even if I copy `.git-completion.bash` locally I'm not sure what to change and I suspect others would find this feature useful.

title: omz update does not update when installed as a submodule
body:
### Describe the bug

OhMyZsh is unable to update a repo in a detached HEAD state, a default for submodules.

As discussed [there](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/discussions/10547#discussioncomment-2657820), `local_head` is checking $branch, which has a default value, instead of `HEAD` file.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a git repo
2. Add OhMyZsh as a submodule with an old commit
3. Declare this in your .zshrc
```
zstyle ':omz:update' mode reminder
zstyle ':omz:update' frequency 1
source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh
```
5. Launch a terminal
6. OMZ should ask for an update
7. Launch update
8. An update is run, but nothing is kept

### Expected behavior

1. Launch a terminal
2. OMZ should ask for an update
3. Launch update
4. An update is run and new contents are kept
5. Launching a new terminal does not trigger the update warning

author: tsengf

title: brew plugin should not call brew shellenv if brew is already on the PATH.
body:
### Describe the bug

This PR change https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/pull/10874 has caused the `brew` plugin to always call `eval $(brew shellenv)` regardless if the environment is already configured correctly. This has unintended consequences, for instance the brew bin directory (`/usr/local/bin` on MacOS Intel) is exported in `PATH` twice.

I would suggest this plugin mimic the behaviour of the brew [installer.sh](https://github.com/Homebrew/install/blob/eb943a56afc66244ff216a511058a89570440610/install.sh#L1017) to only require `eval $(brew shellenv)` if `brew` is not found in the path.

One other thing to note is the brew installer post-install steps says to modify `~/.zprofile` and not `~/.zshrc`. Perhaps this plugin should do what `pyenv` plugin does and [warn if the user did not run the post-install steps](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/7dcabbe6826073ef6069c8a4b6f9a943f00d2df0/plugins/pyenv/pyenv.plugin.zsh#L1-L20) so that it may be called from non interactive scripts.

Other notes regarding the other environment variables exported by `shellenv`, specifically for MacOS Intel:

- `MANPATH` and `INFOPATH` are not required to add `/usr/local/share/man` and `/usr/local/share/info` - they are available in the search path by default.

- Neither is `HOMEBREW_PREFIX`, `HOMEBREW_CELLAR` and `HOMEBREW_REPOSITORY` required by brew to be exported. The `brew` command will ignore these environment variables and recalculate their values. Users should generally prefer `$(brew --prefix)` and not rely on or require `HOMEBREW_PREFIX` to be exported.



### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable plugin `brew`

2. `echo $PATH | tr ':' '\n'`

3. See `/usr/local/bin` listed twice

### Expected behavior

`PATH` should have `/usr/local/bin` listed once

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

MacOS Intel 12.6 Monterey

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

7dcabbe6

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

When there is flag emoji in the prompt and I am halfway through typing a command and I try to autocomplete it with <TAB>, the first word (space-delimited) of my half-baked command is duplicated and remains in the prompt even if try to I delete/backspace (I'm on MacOS) it away.

Before the tab your prompt and unfinished command should be `🇬🇧  vim app`. After the tab your prompt will be 
```

app.py   app2.py  # These are the zsh suggestions
```

author: cloudsftp

title: systemctl plugin should use edit --full
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

systemd

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

The default option for `systemctl edit <unit-name>` is to open an editor with the existing document completely commented out, and upon saving, the new document replaces the old one. This seems like the opposite of what most people would expect from a command named "edit". If you want the behaviour that I find makes more sense (simply open the file for editing and then save it afterwards) you need to run `systemctl edit --full <unit-name>`.

### Describe the proposed feature.

I propose that the default behaviour of the `sc-edit` alias in the systemd plugin should be changed to add --full by default, or at the very least add a second alias which has full by default (perhaps `sc-editf` or `sc-edit-full` .. I prefer the shorter one but the longer one is more descriptive, I suppose, though "full" is not the most descriptive word for what is actually happening).

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

I was going to create a PR for this feature, but looking at the systemd.plugin.zsh file the aliases are (mostly) created algorithmically, so edit would have to be removed from the regular list and added to one of the exceptional aliases (the ones that include --now).

### Related Issues

_No response_

title: Postgres on Apple Silicon: Homebrew uses wrong path when installing PostgreSQL
body:
### Describe the bug

**Mistakenly opened this issue. Sorry, I can not reproduce the problem as described below. Instead, I changed the described path myself, accidentally, which caused the error.** 

---

As described in #10612 and #10764 , I could reproduce the issue of a wrong path when installing PostgreSQL using `$ brew install postgresql` on an Apple silicon machine. A quick fix is described below!


(This is my very first issue. I hope it is helpful to others. Please give me feedback, if I should report it differently or if I was missing anything.) 

### Steps to reproduce

In the terminal, run:

1. $ brew install postgresql
2. $ open ~/.zshrc
3. $ postgres

### Expected behavior

In .zshrc I expected to find `export PGDATA=/opt/homebrew/var/postgresql@14`, instead there was `export PGDATA=/usr/local/var/postgres`

Trying to run PostgreSQL returns the error `postgres: could not access directory "/usr/local/var/postgres": No such file or directory `

Fix: open .zshrc and replace the export with `export PGDATA=/opt/homebrew/var/postgresql@14`

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 12.2.1 (21D62)

### Zsh version

5.8

Terminal.app

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

Git plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

_No response_

### Describe the proposed feature.

Easily switch to the git account & ssh key when using the server, and use different accounts to manipulate the project on the server.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Hi, I'm the author of the [Git_SSH-Account_Switch](https://github.com/tw-yshuang/Git_SSH-Account_Switch), I create a tool that can easy to switch the ssh account for the Github with `git-acc` command, I think this tool may contribute for `ohmyzsh`, I saw @mcornella was replied lots of issues & features that related with `git`, would you mind measuring my tool is a useful feature to contribute to this repo?

### Additional context

# [Git_SSH-Account_Switch](https://github.com/tw-yshuang/Git_SSH-Account_Switch)
please go to checkout the above link

```shell
        +---------------+
        |    git-acc    |
        +---------------+

SYNOPSIS

  git-acc [account]|[option]

OPTIONS

  [account]               use which accounts on this shell, type the account name that you register.
  -h, --help              print help information.
  -add, --add_account     build git_account info. & ssh-key.
      -t, --type          ssh-key types, follow `ssh-keygen` rule, 
                          types: dsa | ecdsa | ecdsa-sk | ed25519 | ed25519-sk | rsa(default)
  -rm, --remove_account   remove git_account info. & ssh-key from this device
  -out, --logout          logout your current ssh-acc.


EXAMPLES

  $ git-acc tw-yshuang
```

author: tw-yshuang

title: Don't use abbreviations for git alias flags
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

Git

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I often lookup oh-my-zsh aliases as follows:

```zsh
alias | grep git
alias | grep "git commit"
```

Here are the results of the latter command:

```
gcas='git commit -a -s'
gcasm='git commit -a -s -m'
gcmsg='git commit -m'
'gcn!'='git commit -v --no-edit --amend'
```

I'm wondering: What is the `-a` flag? What is the `-s` flag? I have to separately go and lookup this information.

### Describe the proposed feature.

I'd like aliases to stay away from using abbreviated flags, and to use the verbose versions instead where available. I'd like to implement this feature for the git plugin, at least, if this feature is desired.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I think it's possible that there's a blind spot in my workflow as stated above; please let me know if that's the case!

### Additional context

_No response_

author: patrikkaprinay

title: omz update opens a new shell
body:
### Describe the bug

every morning i run this script to update my mac. it is one of many scripts i run from a master script. there are scripts that run before and after this script. i call it `zsh_setup.sh` 
```sh
#!/bin/sh

[[ $SHELL == *"zsh" ]] || {
  echo "$(date) : $(hostname -f) : $(basename $0) : Changing default shell to zsh for $USER"
  chsh -s /bin/zsh
}

[ -d $HOME/.oh-my-zsh ] || sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"

[ -d ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/themes/powerlevel10k ] || {
  echo "$(date) : $(hostname -f) : $(basename $0) : Installing powerlevel10k"
  git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k.git ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/themes/powerlevel10k
}

[ -d ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/themes/powerlevel10k ] && {
echo "$(date) : $(hostname -f) : $(basename $0) : Updating powerlevel10k"
git -C ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/themes/powerlevel10k pull
}

[ -f $HOME/.zshrc ] && sed -i 's,^ZSH_THEME.*,ZSH_THEME="powerlevel10k/powerlevel10k",g' $HOME/.zshrc

zsh -c '. ~/.zshrc; omz update'
```

the thing is `omz update` opens a new shell if there is an actual update. which interrupts the flow of my master script.

how do i get `omz update` to exit and return control to the master script?

### Steps to reproduce

1. write script `master.sh`
2. have commands before and after zsh_setup.sh
3. watch when an actual update interrupts flow

### Expected behavior

master.sh continues on its happy path

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

mac 12.6

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Terminal emulator

iterm2

title: New z plugin does not work by placing it into ~/.zshrc → plugins=(... z) array
body:
### Describe the bug

At this moment [oh-my-zsh plugin docs](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/wiki/Plugins) saying that any plugin listed is enabled by listing it in `plugins=(...)` array.

Doing so with [new version of z](https://github.com/agkozak/zsh-z) causes:

```sh
zsh: command not found: z
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Upgrade to latest oh-my-zsh
2. in .zshrc put **z** into **plugins** array
3. reload terminal
4. navigate with **z**
5. `zsh: command not found: z`

### Expected behavior

I expect **z** to work once placed into **plugins** array.

### Screenshots and recordings

My `.zshrc`:

```sh
# Oh My Zsh
export ZSH=$HOME/.oh-my-zsh # Path to oh-my-zsh installation.
export ZSH_COMPDUMP=$ZSH/cache/.zcompdump-$HOST # Store zsh's completion cache inside oh-my-zsh cache folder

# Oh My Zsh config variables
ZSH_THEME=half-life
ENABLE_CORRECTION=true
ZSH_CUSTOM=~/.config/oh-my-zsh-custom # my oh-my-zsh customization folder. contains aliases, functions, plugins, etc.

plugins=(z)

# Init Oh My Zsh after its config variables are set
source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh
```

### OS / Linux distribution

MacOS Monterey 12.6 (21G115)

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (0066280)

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### Additional context

**[agkozak](https://github.com/agkozak)** version of **z** installation section mentions to use a plugin manager to install it.
So **plugins** approach won't work with it and to install new **z** a plugin manager needs to be used?

author: pappalar

title: The `title` function in lib/termsupport.zsh leads to un-deletable characters
body:
### Describe the bug

Window and tab titles are changed by emitting an unprintable escape sequence to the terminal. These escape sequences do not play nicely with the TAB character on multiple terminal emulators—they create un-deletable characters on the first line after command execution.

### Steps to reproduce

This is how I originally noticed the issue:
1. Run `dash` (yes, I know this isn't even zsh anymore, but I promise this is oh-my-zsh's fault)
2. Type `echo $` and then press TAB
3. Attempt to delete all the characters on the current line with the Backspace key

I provide an easier method to reproduce below that stays in zsh.

### Expected behavior

The entire line is cleared.

### Actual behavior
The line contains un-deletable characters.

Note that this only happens on the very first input line—after that things are back to normal.

### Screenshots and recordings

![dash-example](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1699779/199932711-f9b9e85e-8367-4aad-9d1f-c6171953d66d.gif)


### OS / Linux distribution

Windows 10, Ubuntu 22.04, Mac OS 12.6

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

master (b3b336b)

### Terminal emulator

xfce4-terminal, Windows Terminal, tmux, Terminal.app, and iTerm

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

WSL1

### Additional context

I've narrowed down the cause to be the following `case` statement in `lib/termsupport.zsh`: https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/b3b336b0f95644ceda7758871191cdf040b897b2/lib/termsupport.zsh#L19-L38

It's the `print`-ing of the escape sequences that leads to the issue.

I've confirmed that this issue occurs on xfce4-terminal, Windows Terminal, tmux, Terminal.app, and iTerm, so every case in the `title` function leads to this result. I've found that a simple `print -Pn "\r"` at the end works—`title` is only called before drawing the prompt and before executing a command, so in theory we should always be at the start of the line anyway.

## A simpler method to trigger the bug

I noticed later that the following also works:
1. Run `cat`
2. Type `asdf` and then press TAB 
3. Attempt to delete all the characters on the current line with the Backspace key

![cat-example](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1699779/199934432-edf90dd0-9e8a-49b0-88bd-c9ab46d6ec86.gif)


## My probably-incorrect theory for why this is happening

1. The `title` function in `lib/termsupport.zsh` sends one or more escape sequences to the terminal
2. The terminal interprets the unprintable text as printable text when calculating tab stops
3. Pressing TAB causes the terminal to miscalculate where the cursor/margin is

This would explain why "\r" makes things right again, but I could be way off. The most interesting thing to me is how *consistent* every terminal emulator I've tried is—either they all are doing the same incorrect calculation, or my root cause theory is wrong.

title: zrecompile race condition when opening several terminals at once
body:
### Describe the bug

I use the i3 window manager.  I have autologin configured and automatically start six terminals (alacritty) with zsh on the start of i3.  In several of those terminals I often get

``` zrecompile:zcompile:99: invalid zwc file: /home/w/.zcompdump-huette-5.9.zwc ```

I suspect that it is some kind of race condition when multiple processes try to update the zwc file at once.

### Steps to reproduce

It can be reproduced more or less reliably with (something like) the following:

``` touch ~/.zcompdump-${HOST}-5.9; alacritty&; alacritty&; alacritty& ```

When I run the previous command with my usual configuration the error appears almost every time in at least one of the terminals spawned.  However, when I create a new user on my machine with a minimal configuration, then it happens far less often.  But it does happen.

It may be harder to reproduce on more recent hardware.  I am working on an about 10 years old system with an "Intel(R) Celeron(R) CPU N3150 @ 1.60GHz".

```python
# Generate Strong Random Passwords
import random

import django

# This script will generate an 18 character password

components = [string.ascii_letters, string.digits, "!@#$%&"]

chars = []
for clist in components:
    for item in clist:

def generate_password():
    # Store the generated password
    password = []
    # Choose a random item from 'chars' and add it to 'password'
    for i in range(word_length):
        rchar = random.choice(chars)
        password.append(rchar)
    # Return the composed password as a string
    return "".join(password)


# Output generated password
print(generate_password())

```

6. Run the command 
```shell
 cless test_script.py
```


### Expected behavior

The expected behavior should be an output similar to :

```shell
 ccat test_script.py | less
```

author: xandris

title: Plugin: zsh-script-prefix
body:
Hello,

just wanted to share a custom plugin which prefixes a local script file name. 

Say you have typed a script name and forgot to add `./` in front:

```bash
$ somescript.sh
```

By pressing the <kbd>esc</kbd> key once, you will have the same command with `./` prefixed without typing:

```bash
$ ./somescript.sh
```

It's a stripped down version of [plugins/sudo](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/tree/master/plugins/sudo)

You'll find the plugin here: [tmiland/zsh-script-prefix](https://github.com/tmiland/zsh-script-prefix)

Hope someone will find it useful. 🙏‍

_Please disregard if posted in the wrong place._
author: tmiland

title: Support for sso-session in AWS plugin
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

aws plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

New `sso-session` logic was introduced in `aws-cli` 2.9.0: [Add support for configuration of SSO sessions](https://github.com/aws/aws-cli/commit/a019617cb42f76a8ee657f9bc5343b2215c84b63). The way to configure SSO profiles has changed and the [official documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-sso.html) has not yet been updated.

Example config (old method):
```
[profile my-test-profile]
sso_start_url = https://foobar.awsapps.com/start
sso_region = us-east-1
sso_account_id = 123456789012
sso_role_name = restricted-admin-role
region = us-east-1
```

Now, the concept of `sso-session`s have been introduced:
```
[profile restricted-admin-role-123456789012]
sso_session = my-test-test
sso_account_id = 123456789012
sso_role_name = restricted-admin-role
region = us-east-1
output = json

[sso-session my-test-session]
sso_start_url = https://foobar.awsapps.com/start
sso_region = us-east-1
sso_registration_scopes = sso:account:access

[sso-session my-other-test-session]
sso_start_url = https://foobar.awsapps.com/start
sso_region = us-east-1
sso_registration_scopes = sso:account:access
```

Here's the relevant PR: https://github.com/aws/aws-cli/pull/7364

### Describe the proposed feature.

Add support for the new configuration format.

Using the examples above, `asp restricted-admin-role-123456789012 login` should attempt, by default, to use the session defined in `sso_session`, `my-test-session`.

To support multiple sessions for the same profile, add support for the new `--sso-session` option present in `awscli`:
`asp restricted-admin-role-123456789012 login --sso-session my-other-test-session`

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

As documented in the linked PR, I am still able to login using the old profile configuration format.

### Related Issues

_No response_

author: sivavarma111

title: A proposed idea for the networking items
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

## Networking

I really like the items you have for checking different network items, I am building a modular script called [ezsh](https://github.com/girls-whocode/ezsh) that takes many different items that I have used throughout my SA roles, and finding others on the net. I came across yours and would like to add many of your functions to my script as well.

## My changes

I am trying to make this system as functional compact as possible, so here is what I did with your script. (I may change it to use case instead of if, but everything works great.

```sh
#!/bin/zsh
# https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/plugins/systemadmin/systemadmin.plugin.zsh

function retlog() {
  if [[ -z ${1} ]];then
    if [[ -f /var/log/nginx/access.log ]]; then
      echo '/var/log/nginx/access.log'
    elif [[ -f /var/log/httpd/access.log ]]; then
      echo '/var/log/httpd/access.log'
    elif [[ -f /var/log/apache/access.log ]]; then
      echo '/var/log/apache/access.log'
    else
      printf "%s" "Could not find web server access log, specify its location"
      exit 127
    fi
  else
    echo ${1}
  fi
}

ip() {
  # gather external ip 4 address
  if [[ "${1}" == "external4" ]] || [[ "${1}" == "external" ]]; then
    curl -s -S -4 https://icanhazip.com
  # gather external ip 6 address
  elif [[ "${1}" == "external6" ]]; then
    curl -s -S -6 https://icanhazip.com
  # determine local IP address(es)
  elif [[ "${1}" == "internal" ]]; then
    if (( ${+commands[ip]} )); then
      ip addr | awk '/inet /{print $2}' | command grep -v 127.0.0.1
    else
      ifconfig | awk '/inet /{print $2}' | command grep -v 127.0.0.1
    fi
  else
    if [ -t 1 ]; then
      command ip -c "$@"
    else
      command ip "$@"
    fi
  fi
}

# Sort connection state
connections() {
  local ezsh_conn_count
  if [[ ${1} == "80" ]]; then
    # View all 80 Port Connections
    netstat -nat|grep -i ":80"|wc -l
  elif [[ ${1} == "404" ]]; then
    # Statistical connections 404
    awk '($9 ~/404/)' "$(retlog[@])" | awk '{print $9,$7}' | sort
  elif [[ ${1} == "ip" ]]; then
    # On the connected IP sorted by the number of connections
    netstat -ntu | awk '{print $5}' | cut -d: -f1 | sort | uniq -c | sort -n
  elif [[ ${1} == "req" ]]; then
    # top20 of Find the number of requests on 80 port
    if [[ "${2}" == "" ]]; then
      ezsh_conn_count=20
    else
      ezsh_conn_count=${2}
    fi
    netstat -anlp|grep 80|grep tcp|awk '{print $5}'|awk -F: '{print $1}'|sort|uniq -c|sort -nr|head -n ${ezsh_conn_count}
  elif [[ ${1} == "tcp4" ]]; then
    # top20 of Using tcpdump ip 4 port 80 access to view
    if [[ "${2}" == "" ]]; then
      ezsh_conn_count=20
    else
      ezsh_conn_count=${2}
    fi
    curl -s -S -4 https://icanhazip.com
    sudo tcpdump -i eth0 -tnn dst port 80 -c 1000 | awk -F"." '{print $1"."$2"."$3"."$4}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr |head -n ${ezsh_conn_count}
  elif [[ ${1} == "tcp6" ]]; then
    # top20 of Using tcpdump ip 6 port 80 access to view
    if [[ "${2}" == "" ]]; then
      ezsh_conn_count=20
    else
      ezsh_conn_count=${2}
    fi
    curl -s -S -6 https://icanhazip.com
    sudo tcpdump -i eth0 -tnn dst port 80 -c 1000 | awk -F"." '{print $1"."$2"."$3"."$4}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr |head -n ${ezsh_conn_count}
  elif [[ ${1} == "wait" ]]; then
    # top20 of Find time_wait connection
    if [[ "${2}" == "" ]]; then
      ezsh_conn_count=20
    else
      ezsh_conn_count=${2}
    fi
    netstat -n|grep TIME_WAIT|awk '{print $5}'|sort|uniq -c|sort -rn|head -n ${ezsh_conn_count}
  elif [[ ${1} == "syn" ]]; then
    # top20 of Find SYN connection
    if [[ "${2}" == "" ]]; then
      ezsh_conn_count=20
    else
      ezsh_conn_count=${2}
    fi
    netstat -an | grep SYN | awk '{print $5}' | awk -F: '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr|head -n ${ezsh_conn_count}
  elif [[ ${1} == "proc" ]]; then
    # Printing process according to the port number
    netstat -ntlp | grep "${1:-.}" | awk '{print $7}' | cut -d/ -f1
  elif [[ ${1} == "access" ]]; then
    # top10 of gain access to the ip address
    if [[ "${2}" == "" ]]; then
      ezsh_conn_count=10
    else
      ezsh_conn_count=${2}
    fi
    awk '{counts[$(11)]+=1}; END {for(url in counts) print counts[url], url}' "$(retlog[@])"
  elif [[ ${1} == "visits" ]]; then
    # top20 of Most Visited file or page
    if [[ "${2}" == "" ]]; then
      ezsh_conn_count=20
    else
      ezsh_conn_count=${2}
    fi
    awk '{print $11}' "$(retlog[@])"|sort|uniq -c|sort -nr|head -n ${ezsh_conn_count}
  elif [[ ${1} == "pages" ]]; then
    # top100 of Page lists the most time-consuming (more than 60 seconds) as well as the corresponding page number of occurrences
    if [[ "${2}" == "" ]]; then
      ezsh_conn_count=100
    else
      ezsh_conn_count=${2}
    fi
    awk '($NF > 60 && $7~/\.php/){print $7}' "$(retlog[@])" |sort -n|uniq -c|sort -nr|head -n ${ezsh_conn_count}
  elif [[ ${1} == "traffic" ]]; then
    # Website traffic statistics (G)
    awk "{sum+=$10} END {print sum/1024/1024/1024}" "$(retlog[@])"
  elif [[ ${1} == "status" ]]; then
    # Statistical http status.
    awk '{counts[$(9)]+=1}; END {for(code in counts) print code, counts[code]}' "$(retlog[@])"
  else
    netstat -nat | awk '{print $6}'|sort|uniq -c|sort -rn
  fi
}
```

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

### Describe the proposed feature.

With the code written that way, I now have 2 commands `ip` and `connection`, with an added argument

ip external || ip external4 || ip external6 || ip a, etc
connection [[options]]

I hope you like it, and are free to use it if you like.

```zsh
gbm: aliased to git branch -m
gbM: aliased to git branch -M
gbnm: aliased to git branch --no-merged
gbr: aliased to git branch --remote
gbl: aliased to git blame -b -w
gbsb: aliased to git bisect bad
gbsg: aliased to git bisect good
gbsr: aliased to git bisect reset
gbss: aliased to git bisect start
```
Is there any plugin that can provide this functionality?

I'm familiar with the `_expand_alias` flag but it provides alias expansion ***in*** the command line but not in the suggestions. My concern is related to alias expansion in the suggestions.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Option to expand all the aliases which are provided in the suggestions.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

`zsh-completions` or `zsh-autocomplete` might be able to provide this functionality but I haven't found anything related to this in their documentation. :(

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

_No response_
author: PROxZIMA

title: Check if git is installed before relying on it for autoupdate
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

macOS updates frequently break the command line developer tools, so `git` becomes unavailable. This means that when the update check tries to use `git rev-parse`, it gets a non-zero return code, which is incorrectly interpreted as `git` complaining that the directory isn't a git repository. 

The message `"[oh-my-zsh] Can't update: not a git repository."` is then unnecessarily confusing. This has caused me to trip over multiple times—wading through various issues of "why is the `.git` directory missing?" (it wasn't) before stumbling across the solution when I ran `git` and saw the message it gave.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Add a check before calling `git rev-parse` to see if the `git` command is available. Ideally this would also give concrete steps on how to fix the error in the most common case(s). Something along the lines

```
if ! (git --version &>/dev/null); then
    echo >&2 "[oh-my-zsh] Can't update: git is not installed."
    if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        echo >&2 '[oh-my-zsh] Try using "xcode-select --install" to reinstall the Command-Line Developer Tools'
    fi
    return
fi
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

`git grep` shows a similar message is emitted by the Git plugin; it would be nice if this check could be done once globally, and the Git plugin disabled if `git` isn't installed.

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

#11309 and #10861 have both encountered this.
author: edbennett

title: pipenv completion not working
body:
### Describe the bug

The pipenv plugin currently uses the following code to activate completions for pipenv:

    _pipenv() {
      eval $(env COMMANDLINE="${words[1,$CURRENT]}" _PIPENV_COMPLETE=complete-zsh pipenv)
    }

This does not seem to work, mainly because the `_PIPENV_COMPLETE` variable is not being initialized correctly, hence produces no completion functions. According to the [documentation](https://pipenv.pypa.io/en/latest/advanced/#shell-completion) the correct setting is `_PIPENV_COMPLETE=zsh_source`.

Perhaps this is a recent change to pipenv, I'm not sure.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable pipenv plugin, install pipenv
2. From a shell, type pipenv, then TAB

### Expected behavior

Completions should appear for pipenv, namely:

```
$ pipenv<TAB>
check         -- Checks for PyUp Safety security vulnerabilities and against PEP 508 markers provided in Pipfile.
clean         -- Uninstalls all packages not specified in Pipfile.lock.
graph         -- Displays currently-installed dependency graph information.
...

```

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 13.1

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

master (9c2d1af8)

### Terminal emulator

Terminal.app

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

Unfortunately I was not able to fix this by changing to the correct value for `_PIPENV_COMPLETE`. Instead I was able to get it working by removing the _pipenv function entirely and replacing with `eval "$(_PIPENV_COMPLETE=zsh_source pipenv)"` as described in the pipenv doco.

I am using pipenv version 2022.12.19.

title: __git_prompt_git is abysmally slow in WSL2
body:
### Describe the bug

WSL2 mounts windows FS as a remote one, so access to it is very slow. This causes linux `git` to take an unbearably long time to do anything. A common workaround for this is declaring a `git` function in `.profile` that reroutes everything to windows' native `git.exe` if cwd is `/mnt/[cde]/*`. OMZ currently explicitly prohibits this workaround from working with the prompt via the __git_prompt_git function, causing the prompt to take a dozen of seconds to appear when inside git repos.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Be on WSL2
2. Use a theme that displays git info inside the prompt
3. Try to navigate into a git repo inside windows' FS

It would be extremely nice if OMZ could somehow allow customization to this behavior so that I can get the prompt to use `git.exe` without manually patching the `lib/git.zsh` file.

author: RanceH

title: install.sh creates ~/.zshrc file regardless of ZSH variable
body:
### Describe the bug

According to the README, custom .zshrc file locations should be specifed by setting the `ZSH` environment variable.
But the `install.sh` script ignores the `ZSH` variable, creating a `~/.zshrc` file in the user's home directory when it shouldn't.
See: https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/65a1e4edbe678cdac37ad96ca4bc4f6d77e27adf/tools/install.sh#L342


### Steps to reproduce

1. Start with a clean Linux install with zsh shell and non-root user $USER
2. Delete the file `$HOME/.zshrc` if it exists
3. Create a dotfiles directory: `mkdir -p $HOME/dotfiles`
4. Set the ZSH environment variable: `export ZSH=$HOME/dotfiles/omz`
5. Run the ZSH install script according to the README: `sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"`
6. Note that the install script has created the file `$HOME/.zshrc` even though ZSH is set to a different directory
7. Note also that the repo has been pulled down as expected to `$HOME/dotfiles/omw`, it's just that the .zshrc file is in the wrong place

### Expected behavior

The `install.sh` script should follow the ZSH environment variable as specified in the README instructions; it shouldn't put the `.zshrc` file at the root of the user's home directory when the user specifies a different directory.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Debian Testing

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (7de5584)

### Terminal emulator

Kitty

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

## What I Tried
I want to follow the freedesktop/XDG specs and store oh-my-zsh's configuration files (including `.zshrc`) in a directory called `$USER/.config/zsh/oh-my-zsh`. According to the oh-my-zsh README, I can do this by setting a `ZSH` environment variable for the install script.

I set the ZSH environment variable:
`export ZSH=$HOME/.config/zsh/oh-my-zsh`

I make sure a parent directory exists. This is important as, because I'm using the ZSH environment variable, I don't expect this file to exist or ever be created at this location.
`mkdir -p ~/.config/zsh`

Then I make sure my custom directory does *not* exist (otherwise the *main()* function in *install.sh* complains, as expected):
`rm -rf ~/.config/zsh/oh-my-zsh`

I also make sure that no ~/.zshrc file(s) exist:
`rm ~/.zshrc*`

Then I run the script (using a file rather than inline w/ curl):
`sh install.sh`

**Doing an `ls -la` shows that a `~/.zshrc` file has been created in my home directory, not in the directory specified by the ZSH environment variable.**

## Other Checks
I went through and added `echo` statements throughout my copy of the install script. I'm pretty sure my HOME, ZSH, and all other relevant variables are set correctly.

### Describe the bug

The `diff` command does not have any tab file completion for second filename (hypothetical `file_b.txt` in `diff file_a.txt file_b.txt`) though the first filename is completed fine. When I add `unalias diff` to my `.zshrc` file and reload the terminal, or when I start to run `command diff file_a.txt file_b.txt`, tab completion works as expected for `file_b.txt`. Though I am using the powerlevel10k theme, the problem persists even with it commented out of my .zshrc file.

### Steps to reproduce

### Additional context

My `.zshrc` file is as follows:

```zsh
# Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt. Should stay close to the top of ~/.zshrc.

# confirmations, etc.) must go above this block; everything else may go below.

  source "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh"
fi

fi

# Uncomment the following line to display red dots whilst waiting for completion.

## Misc

unsetopt SHARE_HISTORY # prevent shared history between tabs

zstyle ':completion:*' special-dirs false

# To customize prompt, run `p10k configure` or edit ~/.p10k.zsh.
```

title: `geteip` command not working for IPv6
body:
### Describe the bug

The shell function `geteip` from the plugin [`systemadmin`](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/tree/master/plugins/systemadmin) returns the external IP address of the machine.

```bash
$ which geteip
geteip () {
        curl -s -S -4 https://icanhazip.com
        curl -s -S -6 https://icanhazip.com
}

$ geteip
123.123.123.123
curl: (7) Couldn't connect to server
```

When called, it makes a cURL request to <https://icanhazip.com> to get the IP address, first using IPv4 (which succeeds), second using IPv6 (which fails.) It seems the server isn't configured to handle IPv6. Therefore, the IPv6 request fails with : `curl: (7) Couldn't connect to server`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable plugin `systemadmin`.
2. Run `geteip`.


### Expected behavior

I would expect the `geteip` command not to fail. The command should not display an error message or fail with a non-zero status code.
Ideally, I think there should be two commands `geteipv4` and `geteipv6`, and a third command `geteip` for backwards compatibility which should intelligently default to one or the other.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Ubuntu 20.04 / Windows 10 (WSL2)

### Zsh version

5.8

### Oh My Zsh version

master (61dd368)

### Terminal emulator

Windows Terminal (1.15.2874.0)

curl version 7.68.0

title: Git Plugin: alias for `git rebase -i $(git merge-base HEAD $(git_main_branch))`
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

Git

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I often run this command:

```bash
git rebase -i $(git merge-base HEAD master)
```

The use case is for evaluating the commits I've made on dev branch before opening a pull request.

I'd like an alias! I think others might too!

### Describe the proposed feature.

In oh-my-zsh, we have the `$(git_main_branch)` util, so the equivalent line in the plugin file would be:

```bash
alias ??='git rebase -i $(git merge-base HEAD $(git_main_branch))'
```

I'm not sure what the best name would be; here are some suggestions:

- `grbmbm`: git rebase merge-base main/master
- `grbpr`: git rebase (before a) pull-request
- `grbmr`: gitlab flavor of the above
- `grbeek`: git rebase; "eek," my work needs to be fixed up
- `grbbb`: git rebase merge base

I think my personal favorite is `grbpr`, but I'll try it on in my personal config for a big and can report back.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Do other people do this? I'm not sure. Maybe this isn't a commonly used git operation.

### Additional context

_No response_

title: Respect `$ZDOTDIR` in uninstaller
body:
### Describe the bug

Uninstaller does not respect the actual install directory (#10537) and it won't respect the .zshrc location if installed on a different directory using `$ZDOTDIR`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create ZDOTDIR directory if not yet created: `mkdir -p ~/.config/zsh`
2. Install OMZ in custom `ZDOTDIR`: `ZDOTDIR=~/.config/zsh sh install.sh`
3. Run `uninstall_oh_my_zsh`
4. .zshrc and `$ZSH` folder are not removed

### Expected behavior

- Correct OMZ folder is removed
- .zshrc file is looked for in `$ZDOTDIR`

author: mcornella

title: Host installer on install.ohmyz.sh
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

We get a lot of reports of issues accessing the GitHub URL to install OMZ, either because of temporary access issues or even because of outright blocking by GFW (Great Firewall of China).

### Describe the proposed feature.

Self-host installer on a CDN controlled by us, with the following considerations:

1. Automatically deploy the latest `install.sh` script on push events.
2. Make sure the installer is cached by a CDN so that we don't pay the price of bandwidth, as well as to put it close to the user geographically. I think Cloudflare would be good here.
3. Don't redirect to GitHub like in the old days, as it would defeat the purpose of moving from GitHub.
4. Make sure only HTTPS is used.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

To bypass GFW, gitee.com has been tried: https://gitee.com/mcornella/ohmyzsh.
Downsides: it's not hosted by us and is controlled by a Chinese company.

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

_No response_

* * * *


```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [x] Review options for static hosting (Cloudflare, AWS S3, GCP GCS), including pricing and automation
- [x] Review adding security measures such as sig files
```

The FISH shell has this convenient web-based configuration utility. If you install FISH and then type fish_config it will host a web server and bring up a web page where you can customize your prompt and some other things. 

You can select from different prompts and it's really nice to see on the webpage what the prompt will look like ahead of putting it in, and I also really appreciate that if I want to change my prompt, I only have to remember to bring up fish_config, as opposed to looking up config files.

author: banditFox

title: Pipenv plugin: Option to not auto-activate pipenv shell
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

pipenv

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I don't always want to use a pipenv subshell when I cd into a directory with a Pipfile, so I'd like to be able to turn that feature on or off.

This also caused some problems when trying to debug code in VSCode.  As soon as the shell is opened to execute the debugger command, the pipenv subshell is started, and then that new shell just sits and waits for input forever, so the debugging command never actually runs (or at least I need to explicitly exit it first).

### Describe the proposed feature.

Just like something you can configure in .zshrc, kind of like the options for the `ssh-agent` plugin (`zstyle :omz:plugins:ssh-agend lazy yes`)

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I've worked around the above issues by disabling the plugin, or configuring VSCode to 'hide' the Pipfile when attempting to debug so that the plugin doesn't auto-start the subshell.  Unfortunately, the auto-completion parts are still installed, so if I try to run a pipenv command and hit tab, I get errors

```
$pipenv run test core…(eval):1: command not found: _pipenv 
(eval):1: command not found: _pipenv
(eval):1: command not found: _pipenv
(eval):1: command not found: _pipenv
```

(in this example, I hit tab right after typing "core")

So I'll probably have to uninstall the plugin entirely to fix that (or realistically, tweak the plugin file and disable the auto-activate part), but I still want that autocompletion.  It'd be nice if there were an indicator I could add to my prompt as well, like virtualenv or conda plugins

### Additional context

I would open a PR, but I don't know much about defining options for zstyle so I'd have to go learn about that first.

### Related Issues

_No response_

author: sdondley

title: AWS Plugin: Enable support for SSO Sessions
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

AWS Plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

For working with multiple accounts, users no longer need to perform an `aws sso login` after changing the `AWS_PROFILE`. Instead, you can configure an SSO session in the `~/.aws/config` file:

```
[sso-session my-sso-session]
sso_start_url = https://myapp.awsapps.com/start
sso_region = eu-west-1
sso_registration_scopes = sso:account:access
```

Then login once using `aws sso login --sso-session my-sso-session`. Changing the AWS profile after this won't require another `aws sso login`.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Add a command to the `aws` plugin to allow performing an SSO session login.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Adding an alias manually to the `~/.zshrc`.

### Additional context

[https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/sso/login.html](https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/sso/login.html)

### Related Issues

author: LintonFisher-cnic

title: Git plugin: The action and function of alias `gpristine` differ from those of previous versions
body:
### Describe the bug

Alias `gpristine` was defined before:
```
alias gpristine='git reset --hard && git clean -dffx'
```
now:
```
alias gpristine='git reset --hard && git clean --force -dx'
```

### Steps to reproduce

```
alias | grep gpristine
```

### Expected behavior

`gpristine` should clean all untracked files and directories, making the local repo like the newly cloned one.
However, after the commit 35454190f6aeae9b9f16acdbdd0cf72c32610ee8, this alias have only one `--force` clean option.

> Git will refuse to modify untracked nested git repositories (directories with a .git subdirectory) unless a second `-f` is given.

title: Tab completion duplicates command with multiple theme
body:
### Describe the bug

When trying to use tab-completion to complete file/folder names the already typed command is duplicated. The duplicate  is then the real command, the first/original stays in place and cannot be deleted.

I could track down the issue to the theme, agnoster in my case. I tried a few more but not all...
Affected themes: agnoster, apple, awesomepanda, evan, fox, josh
Unaffected themes: clean, simple, gentoo, mikeh

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open a new shell (home directory)
2. Type ``cd D<Tab>`` or ``cd Do<Tab>`` --> Command is duplicated (see below)
3. Type ``cd Dow<Tab>`` --> Works fine

The behavior seems to happen only when there's more than one candidate for auto-completion and it happens only on the first <Tab>.

### Expected behavior

The command is not duplicated.

### Screenshots and recordings

Before pressing <Tab>:
```
 ✘ chrbrnracn@AMAVNF44  ~  cd Do
```
After pressing <Tab> once:
```
 ✘ chrbrnracn@AMAVNF44  ~  cd Do cd Do
Documents/  Downloads/
```

After pressing <Tab> twice:
```
 ✘ chrbrnracn@AMAVNF44  ~  cd Do cd Documents/
Documents/  Downloads/
```

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 13.2

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

master (f8bf8f0)

### Terminal emulator

Terminal.app

None

### Additional context

.zshrc content (removed comments and empty lines)
```
export PATH=$HOME/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH:/opt/homebrew/bin
export ZSH="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh"
ZSH_THEME="agnoster"
zstyle :omz:plugins:ssh-agent agent-forwarding yes
zstyle :omz:plugins:ssh-agent lazy yes
zstyle :omz:plugins:ssh-agent identities ~/.ssh/id_rsa
plugins=(
  ssh-agent

)
source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh
alias ls="ls --color=always"
alias ll="ls -l"
```

author: briansorahan

title: gnu-utils plugin on MacOS: Problem with "ls=ls -G" alias
body:
### Describe the bug

I am running OMZ! on macOS 11.7.4, and using the `robbyrussell` theme.
On MacOS, while using oh-my-zsh, I noticed an issue with the `ls -la` command that I traced back to an alias that the `gnu-utils` plugin uses for `oh-my-zsh!`. When the `gnu-utils` plugin is disabled, the zsh (default) output of `ls -la /Users` shows both the "user" and "group" ownership, of files listed in the directory. 

This is the default output for zsh on MacOS, and is what SHOULD happen when issuing the same command while using the `gnu-utils` plugin.

HOWEVER, after enabling the `gnu-utils` plugin in "oh-my-zsh!", the same command `ls -la /Users` fails to show the group ownership of files in that directory. It only shows the user ownership.

THE PROBLEM:
In [this stack-exchange issue](https://apple.stackexchange.com/questions/455048/how-to-show-users-and-groups-in-zsh-on-macos?noredirect=1#comment662485_455048) that I posted:
I discovered that the issue came from the line `alias ls=ls -G` alias provided by the `gnu-utils` plugin.

THE SOLUTION:
A stack exchange user by the name of[ fd0](https://apple.stackexchange.com/users/128657/fd0) found the problem and identified that the solution is to either:

1) Place the line `unalias ls` at the end of your `.zshrc` file,

or to

2) Find where OMZ! adds the alias and comment it out

I could not solve solution #2, as I can't figure out where OMZ! adds the alias.

### Steps to reproduce

1) Enable gnu-utils plugin on MacOS
2) Run the command `ls -la`
3) Notice the output is missing group ownership of listed files

EX: gnu-utils PLUGIN ENABLED
```
Danran@Dans-MacbookPro ~$ ls -la /Users
total 0
drwxr-xr-x   5 root  160 Jan  1  2020 .
drwxr-xr-x  20 root  640 Jan  1  2020 ..
-rw-r--r--   1 root    0 Jan  1  2020 .localized
drwxrwxrwt  14 root  448 Feb 16 00:25 Shared
drwxr-xr-x+ 74 Danran  2368 Feb 17 15:32 boo
```

### Expected behavior

1) Enable gnu-utils plugin on MacOS
2) Run the command `ls -la`
3) Notice the output includes the group ownership of listed files

EX: gnu-utils PLUGIN DISABLED
```
Danran@Dans-MacbookPro ~$ ls -la /Users
total 0
drwxr-xr-x   5 root  admin   160 Jan  1  2020 .
drwxr-xr-x  20 root  wheel   640 Jan  1  2020 ..
-rw-r--r--   1 root  wheel     0 Jan  1  2020 .localized
drwxrwxrwt  14 root  wheel   448 Feb 16 00:25 Shared
drwxr-xr-x@ 74 Danran   staff  2368 Feb 17 15:26 boo
```

### Screenshots and recordings

Not Necessary.

macOS

### Zsh version

5.8

### Oh My Zsh version

master (cc73a929)

### Terminal emulator

Terminal.app, iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

author: fferegrino

title: OMZ global alias overrides the Perforce wildcard '...'
body:
### Describe the bug

After installing OMZ, my Perforce commands do not work anymore with the `...` wildcard.
This wildcard is used in Perforce to match directories recursively:
https://www.perforce.com/manuals/p4guide/Content/P4Guide/syntax.syntax.wildcards.html

Instead, this wildcard is replaced with `../..`, which means that Perforce will check only one folder two levels higher.

I know I can disable all directories-related aliases by adding `zstyle ':omz:directories' aliases no` to the .zshrc, but I would like to keep some of them.

Also, I can undefine it by using `unalias \...`, but I thought maybe the best option would be just to make them regular aliases instead of global ones.

### Steps to reproduce

```
> p4 status ...
../.. - no file(s) to reconcile.
```

### Expected behavior

Perforce should scan the current folder, and all the directories below.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 13.2.1

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

master (8c808da)

### Terminal emulator

Terminal.app

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_
author: dgrinin

title: `omz update` stops script execution if updates are performed
body:
### Describe the bug

I wrote a command that does the following:
```bash
update_all () {
  omz update
  brew upgrade
  brew update -y
  mas upgrade
}
```
When `omz update` doesn't have anything to update, the rest of the commands run just fine. When there are omz updates performed, the command stops after the omz updates.

### Steps to reproduce

Setup the following command in your .zshrc file or similar on a MacOS device with Brew and MAS installed:
```bash
update_all () {
  omz update
  brew upgrade
  brew update -y
  mas upgrade
}
```

Run `update_all`. If OMZ has updates, it fails. If it doesn't, the following commands run.

### Expected behavior

All commands should run, regardless of OMZ updates.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

MacOS 13.2.1

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

master (aca0488)

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_
author: dbhagen

title: Plugins not being sourced when using a different $ZSH_CUSTOM path
body:
### Describe the bug

I want to use a different directory for `$ZSH_CUSTOM`. According to [the documentation](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/wiki/Customization#using-another-customization-directory) this should be pretty simple.

But, for some reason my plugins don't immediately work when opening a new shell. It only works after I explicitly `source ~/.zshrc` again. All other customizations (`aliases.zsh`, `functions.zsh`) do work without sourcing.

This happens for all plugins. So not just the custom ones, but even the plugins which `oh-my-zsh` provides like `git` and `yarn`.

I have no clue why this is the case. The `plugins.zsh` file does get loaded when starting a new shell, so that's not the issue. Does anyone know why this does not work?

My `.zshrc` file is:
```
# Path to my oh-my-zsh custom directory
ZSH_CUSTOM="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh-custom"

# Path to my oh-my-zsh installation
export ZSH="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh"

# Source oh-my-zsh
source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh
```

The structure of my `$ZSH_CUSTOM` directory adheres to the conventional file hierarchy. My plugins reside the `$ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins/` directory, and are loaded in a `$ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins.zsh`. My other customizations do work as expected, so without having to `source ~/.zshrc` each time when opening a new shell.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Copy `$ZSH_CUSTOM` directory to desired path.
2. Override original directory in `~/.zshrc` (`ZSH_CUSTOM="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh-custom"`)
3. Open a new shell

### Expected behavior

I would expect the plugins to immediately work when loading a new shell. So without having to `source ~/.zshrc` each time.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 13.2.1

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

9f9d3b7

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_

title: zplug warns `Failed to load vcs_info.zsh`
body:
### Describe the bug

When using
```
zplug robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh, use:"lib/*.zsh"
zplug load --verbose
```
I see the following warning
```
WARNING:  Failed to load "~/.zplug/repos/robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh/lib/vcs_info.zsh" (robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh)
```
This is because the line 
```
autoload -Uz +X regexp-replace VCS_INFO_formats 2>/dev/null || return
```

Somehow the `return` generates exit status 1.

I tested a script with one line `return`, and then source it in the zsh and I also observe exit status 1. This is strange.




### Steps to reproduce

```
zplug robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh, use:"lib/*.zsh"
zplug load --verbose
```

### Expected behavior

Load `vcs_info.zsh` successfully

### Screenshots and recordings

ubuntu 22.04

5.8.1

author: liushapku

title: Tab completion doesn't work if only two directories are present with certain naming
body:
### Describe the bug

In some odd cases if you have only two directories present, e.g. a directory named `c` and a directory named `d` the tab completion will first start off with a capital letter of one of the directories (in the example listed, trying to type `ls` or `cd` and pressing tab will offer `D` and then switch to `d/`)

This behavior happens both inside the directory where the two directories are and from a path outside of the directory.



### Steps to reproduce

1. Create directories begging with `c` and `d` inside an empty directory
2. Try to `cd` or `ls` autocomplete into them
3. You are offered only `d/` as an option

### Expected behavior

You should be offered to choose between the directory starting with `c` and the directory starting with `d`

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Fedora release 37 (Thirty Seven)

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (8a008e1)

### Terminal emulator

GNOME Terminal

None

author: massadm

title: Implement Tailscale Plugin
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

Tailscale

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

_No response_

### Describe the proposed feature.

Tailscale provides a CLI tool, and unfortunately on Mac systems, it doesn't install it to the path.
According to the [official docs](https://tailscale.com/kb/1080/cli/?tab=macos#using-the-cli), it's recommended to add the following alias:
```zsh
alias tailscale="/Applications/Tailscale.app/Contents/MacOS/Tailscale"
```

The feature request is to add a `tailscale` plugin that adds this alias on Mac systems.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

_No response_

title: Add resistance for OS update/upgrade
body:
### Describe the bug

#### I have installed ZSH and everything was working smoothly. After updating my OS, some of the commands are not found anymore. I know there are a bunch of solutions available out there, but I suggest making your own like the universal one. Or maybe there is one, not mentioned in your doc and in the repo. Could you please let us know your decision or plans on this?

---

#### Error text in case of using Go:

```text
zsh: command not found: go
```

If you are using Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS, after updating OS and, for example, just running the go command will give you the same bug.

#### I want ZSH to smoothly adopt system updates.

author: dsha256

title: Changelog improvements
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

OMZ helpfully nudges me to update, and when I do, it lists a lot of things that were changed but it's a chore to get more details.

For instance, the latest update contained this line:

```
- 041c35f [amuse]                Add virtualenv support (#8987)
```

Sounds cool, but how does it work? It links the issue #, but now I have to open a browser, navigate to the project, then nav to its issues, and then lastly nav to #8987. Is it hard? No. Is it time-consuming? When there's 25 issues mentioned (like today), it can really add up!

That's part of the friction. The other part is updates like this:

```
 - cc73a92 [git]                  Add `gpod` alias
```

I understand that not all commits have an issue # to tie them to, but there's a git hash -- I can look at the code changes, but that requires roughly same amount of steps as above.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Add clickable links for each git hash & issue #. I'm making an assumption that most (if not all) of these refs are to the main project; if that's true, then this should be pretty straightforward. If not, then maybe alternatives would need to be considered.

>  - [041c35f](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/commit/041c35f) [amuse]                Add virtualenv support ([#8987](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/pull/8987))
>  - [cc73a92](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/commit/cc73a92) [git]                  Add `gpod` alias



### Describe alternatives you've considered

An alternative to providing this in the terminal would be to publish such and link it from the terminal, but I think having the details available in your terminal would be the easiest to work with.

### Additional context

I apologize if this is rehashing old decisions, I searched the issues (and online) but it seems the only way I can find details on the updated items is to do a ton of manual navigating myself. This seems like such an obvious omission that I can't but think it's intentional for some reason?

### Related Issues

_No response_

title: Red-Prompt on new line instead of replacing green one?
body:
### Describe the bug

When I enter a command, that returns an error, the line with that command is marked with a green arrow.
The next line, valid or not, gets marked red, which is confusing, since it was valid in my case.

### Steps to reproduce

Example: Installing something via apt without using sudo:

❯ apt-get install mosh
E: Could not open lock file /var/lib/dpkg/lock-frontend - open (13: Permission denied)
E: Unable to acquire the dpkg frontend lock (/var/lib/dpkg/lock-frontend), are you root?
❯ sudo apt-get install mosh
[sudo] password for user:

The first line has a green arrow, while the thirds one is red.

### Expected behavior

Color the arrow red where the command actually failed.

### Screenshots and recordings

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/46635221/224000635-d131e0d5-e84d-4acd-ac73-8c6a982c5688.png)


### OS / Linux distribution

Debian 11 (Bullseye) (using Windows Subsystem for Linux)

### Zsh version

5.8

### Terminal emulator

Windows Terminal

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

WSL2

### Additional context

I am using Oh My ZSH with powerlevel10k
author: thxrben

title: Does `LS_COLORS` should as same as `LSCOLORS`?
body:
### Describe the bug

After updated the oh my zsh days ago, I notice the color of `cd`'s autocompletion changed. I have a terminal session that still the old color, after several search and test. I find my `used to color` terminal is `LSCOLORS="Gxfxcxdxbxegedabagacad"` and the `LS_COLORS` is unset. 

The new environment after omz updated is `LSCOLORS=Gxfxcxdxbxegedabagacad` and `LS_COLORS="di=34:ln=35:so=32:pi=33:ex=31:bd=34;46:cd=34;43:su=37;41:sg=30;43:tw=30;42:ow=34;42:"`.

After check GitHub commit history, this [commit]( https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/commit/95d0c4b603e0c880bcf20bc9211b2162e94ed925) set the `LS_COLORS`. 

However, depending this [website](https://geoff.greer.fm/lscolors/) and I tested in my local, the `Gxfxcxdxbxegedabagacad` should be `di=1;36:ln=35:so=32:pi=33:ex=31:bd=34;46:cd=34;43:su=30;41:sg=30;46:tw=30;42:ow=30;43`. 

Should it be sync to each other? 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add `unset LS_COLORS` in `~/.zshrc` and `zstyle ':completion:*' list-colors "${(@s.:.)LS_COLORS}"` make my `used color` back
2. Or `export LS_COLORS="di=1;36:ln=35:so=32:pi=33:ex=31:bd=34;46:cd=34;43:su=30;41:sg=30;46:tw=30;42:ow=30;43"` and `zstyle ':completion:*' list-colors "${(@s.:.)LS_COLORS}"` can sync the directories colors between `cd` autocompletion and the `ls`.

### Expected behavior

1. `LS_COLORS` and `LSCOLORS` are sync
2. Or doesn't change my color. 

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 13.2.1

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (92387d9)

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_

author: Oman395

title: zsh auto package suggestions is disabled
body:
### Describe the bug

#### When we type a command into the Ubuntu bash terminal and instead of going `command not found: <package>`, we get something like this:

```
$ ag 
Command 'ag' not found, but can be installed with:
sudo apt-get install silversearcher-ag
```

#### But in `oh-my-zsh`, this feature of package suggestion is not present by default.

### Steps to reproduce

#### Try to run any package that is not come pre-installed in your system, like

```
$ ag
zsh: command not found: ag
```

### Expected behavior

#### Must recommend package suggestions by default, like this:

```
$ ag
Command 'ag' not found, but can be installed with:
sudo apt-get install silversearcher-ag
```

### Screenshots and recordings

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/97805339/225109888-9bdcd1bd-87e8-4c89-976d-8881c6fc26e4.png)


### OS / Linux distribution

Ubuntu 20.10

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (3ea0e0d)

### Terminal emulator

Tilix

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

#### Solved using the following commands:

```
sudo apt install command-not-found
echo "source /etc/zsh_command_not_found" >> ~/.zshrc
exec zsh
```

[Reference](https://askubuntu.com/questions/1226574/how-do-i-enable-zsh-package-suggestions-as-in-ubuntu-bash)


Signed-off-by: Shubham Kumar [shubham.kumar@ramanujan.du.ac.in](mailto:shubham.kumar@ramanujan.du.ac.in)

body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

Git plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Being able to easily go back and forth between branches. We have `gcm` to easily go to main, but to make it easier to go back to a feature branch it would be nice to have a shortcut for `git checkout -`. This command goes to the previous checked out branch.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Add a new git alias like `gcw` that maps to `git checkout -`. 

### Describe alternatives you've considered

If there is a better shortcut that's fine. I thought `gcp` would make sense but that's already taken so `gcw` (git checkout working) makes sense to me.

title: AWS prompt now always shows when region is set
body:
### Describe the bug

This didn't happen before I updated today (18-01-2023) since before the region was added to prompt.

I have the env var AWS_REGION set to eu-west-2 on profile/zshrc, it now shows 

```<aws:> <region:eu-west-2>```

at the end of my prompt at all times, not just when AWS_PROFILE is set as it behaved before.

Is there any way a configuration/switch could be added in order to determine whether this should be shown? By default, I don't have an AWS_PROFILE set. It would be nice if this was only shown when AWS_PROFILE was set.

Thanks

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable plugin 'aws'
2. `export AWS_REGION=eu-west-2` (or any region)
3. aws prompt will show at end of prompt even if no profile is set

### Expected behavior

Unsure if this is intended, but given the empty space after `<aws:>` I would assume it's not

title: Proxy exclusion
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

shell-proxy

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

When I set my corporate proxy, there's a list of internal domains/URLs, IP where the proxy should not be used. 
If no exclusion is set, the proxy normally returns a `Received HTTP code 502 from proxy after CONNECT` as it's not supposed/configured for handling some internal requests

### Describe the proposed feature.

Shell proxy should also allow the definition of `NO_PROXY` variable, which is the standard in bash shell, and, even better, set it in various softwares (as not always actually correctly read the shell variable for no proxy) 

### Describe alternatives you've considered

So far, I am exporting the proxy exclusion manually and also set it manually in npm and git

### Additional context

_No response_

title: Verbose flag for upgrade.sh
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

The output from upgrade.sh is quite large and overwhelming.

### Describe the proposed feature.

A verbose flag for the upgrade that will limit the amount of text written to the terminal. It be like the ":omz:update" zstyle flags, like `zstyle ':omz:update' verbose silent` where no output but errors are presented, `zstyle ':omz:update' verbose minimal` where  only a couple of lines, like "Updating Oh My Zsh" and "Oh My Zsh is already at the latest version.", are rendered. And then `zstyle ':omz:update' default`, which renders the standard update text.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Editing the tools/update.sh file directly every time I install an update.

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

_No response_
