title: Aliases for Python's Ruff Linter/Formatter
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

Ruff plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I'm now more inclined to use more oh my zsh aliases than to write my own, searching through the plugin list I found quite a few aliases for [pylint](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/tree/master/plugins/pylint) and [PEP8](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/tree/master/plugins/pep8), but I did not see a plugin for [Ruff](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/), a tool that I'm been using more in the last few days.

### Describe the proposed feature.

So to enrich the Python's plugin environment would it be possible to add this alias plugin?

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I've been thinking about writing this plugin and sending it out as a PR. But I do not know if there are more people who would like to have this new feature on oh my zsh.

So aliases that I'm considering are:
| alias  |  command | description |
|---|---|---|
|`rc`|`ruff check`| Lint all files in the current directory |
|`rcf`|`ruff check --fix`| Lint all files in the current directory, and fix any fixable errors |
|`rcw`|`ruff check --watch`| Lint all files in the current directory, and re-lint on change |
|`rf`|`ruff format`| Format all files in the current directory |

The is a much more cli flags that should be supported as we can see it [here](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/configuration/#full-command-line-interface)

NOTE: I did not searched to the plugins list if the `r` or `rc*` aliases interfere with others plugins. This is just a recommendation of what would be nice to have.


### Additional context

I've not found any PR related to this topic.

### Related Issues

I've not found any Issue related to this topic.

title: HistDb update outcome error
body:
### Describe the bug

Hello,

the oh-my-zsh is loaded successfully, but i got an error : zsh: histdb-update-outcome: function definition file not found                            .

Thanks.

### Steps to reproduce

Install the plugin, restart terminal

### Expected behavior

No issue

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macos 13.6.9

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (1d09c6b)

### Terminal emulator

terminal

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

.zshrc file:
```sh
# If you come from bash you might have to change your $PATH.
# export PATH=$HOME/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH

# Path to your oh-my-zsh installation.
export ZSH="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh"

# Set name of the theme to load --- if set to "random", it will
# load a random theme each time oh-my-zsh is loaded, in which case,
# to know which specific one was loaded, run: echo $RANDOM_THEME
ZSH_THEME="agnoster"

# Uncomment the following line to use case-sensitive completion.
# CASE_SENSITIVE="true"

# Uncomment the following line to use hyphen-insensitive completion.
# HYPHEN_INSENSITIVE="true"

# Uncomment to change the auto-update behavior
# zstyle ':omz:update' mode reminder  # just remind me to update when it's time

# Uncomment to display red dots whilst waiting for completion.
# COMPLETION_WAITING_DOTS="true"

# Plugins
plugins=(git zsh-autosuggestions zsh-syntax-highlighting)

# Source Oh-My-Zsh
source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh

# Custom plugin: zsh-histdb
source $HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-histdb/sqlite-history.zsh
autoload -Uz add-zsh-hook
add-zsh-hook precmd histdb-update-outcome

# Setting up fpath and FPATH for functions
export FPATH=$HOME/usr/share/zsh/5.9/functions:$FPATH
fpath=(/usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions /usr/share/zsh/site-functions $fpath)

# Initialize compinit
# Terminal autocomplete fix
autoload -Uz compinit && compinit

plugins=(
    git
    docker
    asdf
    zsh-autosuggestions
    zsh-completions 
    zsh-history-substring-search 
    zsh-syntax-highlighting
)

# Load Angular CLI autocompletion
source <(ng completion script)

# User configuration
# Set personal aliases, overriding those provided by oh-my-zsh libs
# alias zshconfig="mate ~/.zshrc"
# alias ohmyzsh="mate ~/.oh-my-zsh"
`

sqlite-history.zsh:

`which sqlite3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || return;

zmodload zsh/datetime # for EPOCHSECONDS
zmodload zsh/system # for sysopen
builtin which sysopen &>/dev/null || return; # guard against zsh older than 5.0.8.

zmodload -F zsh/stat b:zstat # just zstat
autoload -U add-zsh-hook

typeset -g HISTDB_QUERY=""
if [[ -z ${HISTDB_FILE} ]]; then
    typeset -g HISTDB_FILE="${HOME}/.histdb/zsh-history.db"
else
    typeset -g HISTDB_FILE
fi

typeset -g HISTDB_INODE=()
typeset -g HISTDB_SESSION=""
typeset -g HISTDB_HOST=""
typeset -g HISTDB_INSTALLED_IN="${(%):-%N}"



sql_escape () {
    print -r -- ${${@//\'/\'\'}//$'\x00'}
}

_histdb_query () {
    sqlite3 -batch -noheader -cmd ".timeout 1000" "${HISTDB_FILE}" "$@"
    [[ "$?" -ne 0 ]] && echo "error in $@"
}

_histdb_stop_sqlite_pipe () {
    if [[ -n $HISTDB_FD ]]; then
        if print -nu$HISTDB_FD; then
            exec {HISTDB_FD}>&-;  # https://stackoverflow.com/a/22794374/2639190
        fi
    fi
    # Sometimes, it seems like closing the fd does not terminate the
    # sqlite batch process, so here is a horrible fallback.
    # if [[ -n $HISTDB_SQLITE_PID ]]; then
    #     ps -o args= -p $HISTDB_SQLITE_PID | read -r args
    #     if [[ $args == "sqlite3 -batch ${HISTDB_FILE}" ]]; then
    #         kill -TERM $HISTDB_SQLITE_PID
    #     fi
    # fi
}

add-zsh-hook zshexit _histdb_stop_sqlite_pipe

_histdb_start_sqlite_pipe () {
    local PIPE==(<<<'')
    setopt local_options no_notify no_monitor
    mkfifo $PIPE
    sqlite3 -batch -noheader "${HISTDB_FILE}" < $PIPE >/dev/null &|
    sysopen -w -o cloexec -u HISTDB_FD -- $PIPE
    command rm $PIPE
    zstat -A HISTDB_INODE +inode ${HISTDB_FILE}
}

_histdb_query_batch () {
    local CUR_INODE
    zstat -A CUR_INODE +inode ${HISTDB_FILE}
    if [[ $CUR_INODE != $HISTDB_INODE ]]; then
        _histdb_stop_sqlite_pipe
        _histdb_start_sqlite_pipe
    fi
    cat >&$HISTDB_FD
    echo ';' >&$HISTDB_FD # make sure last command is executed
}

_histdb_init () {
    if [[ -n "${HISTDB_SESSION}" ]]; then
        return
    fi

    if ! [[ -e "${HISTDB_FILE}" ]]; then
        local hist_dir="${HISTDB_FILE:h}"
        if ! [[ -d "$hist_dir" ]]; then
            mkdir -p -- "$hist_dir"
        fi
        _histdb_query <<-EOF
create table commands (id integer primary key autoincrement, argv text, unique(argv) on conflict ignore);
create table places   (id integer primary key autoincrement, host text, dir text, unique(host, dir) on conflict ignore);
create table history  (id integer primary key autoincrement,
                       session int,
                       command_id int references commands (id),
                       place_id int references places (id),
                       exit_status int,
                       start_time int,
                       duration int);
PRAGMA user_version = 2;
EOF
    fi
    if [[ -z "${HISTDB_SESSION}" ]]; then
        ${HISTDB_INSTALLED_IN:h}/histdb-migrate "${HISTDB_FILE}"
        HISTDB_HOST=${HISTDB_HOST:-"'$(sql_escape ${HOST})'"}
        HISTDB_SESSION=$(_histdb_query "select 1+max(session) from history inner join places on places.id=history.place_id where places.host = ${HISTDB_HOST}")
        HISTDB_SESSION="${HISTDB_SESSION:-0}"
        readonly HISTDB_SESSION
    fi

    _histdb_start_sqlite_pipe
    _histdb_query_batch >/dev/null <<EOF
create index if not exists hist_time on history(start_time);
create index if not exists place_dir on places(dir);
create index if not exists place_host on places(host);
create index if not exists history_command_place on history(command_id, place_id);
PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
PRAGMA synchronous=normal;
EOF
}

declare -ga _BORING_COMMANDS
_BORING_COMMANDS=("^ls$" "^cd$" "^ " "^histdb" "^top$" "^htop$")

if [[ -z "${HISTDB_TABULATE_CMD[*]:-}" ]]; then
    declare -ga HISTDB_TABULATE_CMD
    HISTDB_TABULATE_CMD=(column -t -s $'\x1f')
fi

_histdb_update_outcome () {
    local retval=$?
    local finished=$EPOCHSECONDS
    [[ -z "${HISTDB_SESSION}" ]] && return

    _histdb_init
    _histdb_query_batch <<EOF &|
update history set
      exit_status = ${retval},
      duration = ${finished} - start_time
where id = (select max(id) from history) and
      session = ${HISTDB_SESSION} and
      exit_status is NULL;
EOF
}

# Add the hook after defining the function
add-zsh-hook precmd _histdb_update_outcome

_histdb_addhistory () {
    local cmd="${1[0, -2]}"

    for boring in "${_BORING_COMMANDS[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$cmd" =~ $boring ]]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done

    local cmd="'$(sql_escape $cmd)'"
    local pwd="'$(sql_escape ${PWD})'"
    local started=$EPOCHSECONDS
    _histdb_init

    if [[ "$cmd" != "''" ]]; then
        _histdb_query_batch <<EOF &|
insert into commands (argv) values (${cmd});
insert into places   (host, dir) values (${HISTDB_HOST}, ${pwd});
insert into history
  (session, command_id, place_id, start_time)
select
  ${HISTDB_SESSION},
  commands.id,
  places.id,
  ${started}
from
  commands, places
where
  commands.argv = ${cmd} and
  places.host = ${HISTDB_HOST} and
  places.dir = ${pwd}
;
EOF
    fi
    return 0
}

add-zsh-hook zshaddhistory _histdb_addhistory
#add-zsh-hook precmd _histdb_update_outcome
precmd_functions+=(histdb-update-outcome)


histdb-top () {
    _histdb_init
    local sep=$'\x1f'
    local field
    local join
    local table
    1=${1:-cmd}
    case "$1" in
        dir)
            field=places.dir
            join='places.id = history.place_id'
            table=places
            ;;
        cmd)
            field=commands.argv
            join='commands.id = history.command_id'
            table=commands
            ;;;
    esac
    _histdb_query -separator "$sep" \
            -header \
            "select count(*) as count, places.host, replace($field, '
', '
$sep$sep') as ${1:-cmd} from history left join commands on history.command_id=commands.id left join places on history.place_id=places.id group by places.host, $field order by count(*)" | \
        "${HISTDB_TABULATE_CMD[@]}"
}

histdb-sync () {
    _histdb_init

    # this ought to apply to other readers?
    echo "truncating WAL"
    echo 'pragma wal_checkpoint(truncate);' | _histdb_query_batch
    
    local hist_dir="${HISTDB_FILE:h}"
    if [[ -d "$hist_dir" ]]; then
        () {
            setopt local_options no_pushd_ignore_dups

            pushd -q "$hist_dir"
            if [[ $(git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree) != "true" ]] || [[ "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" != "${PWD:A}" ]]; then
                git init
                git config merge.histdb.driver "${HISTDB_INSTALLED_IN:h}/histdb-merge %O %A %B"
                echo "${HISTDB_FILE:t} merge=histdb" >>! .gitattributes
                git add .gitattributes
                git add "${HISTDB_FILE:t}"
            fi
            _histdb_stop_sqlite_pipe # Stop in case of a merge, starting again afterwards
            git commit -am "history" && git pull --no-edit && git push
            _histdb_start_sqlite_pipe
            popd -q
        }
    fi

    echo 'pragma wal_checkpoint(passive);' | _histdb_query_batch
}

histdb () {
    _histdb_init
    local -a opts
    local -a hosts
    local -a indirs
    local -a atdirs
    local -a sessions

    zparseopts -E -D -a opts \
               -host+::=hosts \
               -in+::=indirs \
               -at+::=atdirs \
               -forget \
               -yes \
               -detail \
               -sep:- \
               -exact \
               d h -help \
               s+::=sessions \
               -from:- -until:- -limit:- \
               -status:- -desc

    local usage="usage:$0 terms [--desc] [--host[ x]] [--in[ x]] [--at] [-s n]+* [-d] [--detail] [--forget] [--yes] [--exact] [--sep x] [--from x] [--until x] [--limit n] [--status x]
    --desc     reverse sort order of results
    --host     print the host column and show all hosts (otherwise current host)
    --host x   find entries from host x
    --in       find only entries run in the current dir or below
    --in x     find only entries in directory x or below
    --at       like --in, but excluding subdirectories
    -s n       only show session n
    -d         debug output query that will be run
    --detail   show details
    --forget   forget everything which matches in the history
    --yes      don't ask for confirmation when forgetting
    --exact    don't match substrings
    --sep x    print with separator x, and don't tabulate
    --from x   only show commands after date x (sqlite date parser)
    --until x  only show commands before date x (sqlite date parser)
    --limit n  only show n rows. defaults to $LINES or 25
    --status x only show rows with exit status x. Can be 'error' to find all nonzero."

    local selcols="session as ses, dir"
    local cols="session, replace(places.dir, '$HOME', '~') as dir"
    local where="1"
    if [[ -p /dev/stdout ]]; then
        local limit=""
    else
        local limit="${$((LINES - 4)):-25}"
    fi

    local forget="0"
    local forget_accept=0
    local exact=0

    if (( ${#hosts} )); then
        local hostwhere=""
        local host=""
        for host ($hosts); do
            host="${${host#--host}#=}"
            hostwhere="${hostwhere}${host:+${hostwhere:+ or }places.host='$(sql_escape ${host})'}"
        done
        where="${where}${hostwhere:+ and (${hostwhere})}"
        cols="${cols}, places.host as host"
        selcols="${selcols}, host"
    else
        where="${where} and places.host=${HISTDB_HOST}"
    fi

    if (( ${#indirs} + ${#atdirs} )); then
        local dirwhere=""
        local dir=""
        for dir ($indirs); do
            dir="${${${dir#--in}#=}:-$PWD}"
            dirwhere="${dirwhere}${dirwhere:+ or }places.dir like '$(sql_escape $dir)%'"
        done
        for dir ($atdirs); do
            dir="${${${dir#--at}#=}:-$PWD}"
            dirwhere="${dirwhere}${dirwhere:+ or }places.dir = '$(sql_escape $dir)'"
        done
        where="${where}${dirwhere:+ and (${dirwhere})}"
    fi

    if (( ${#sessions} )); then
        local sin=""
        local ses=""
        for ses ($sessions); do
            ses="${${${ses#-s}#=}:-${HISTDB_SESSION}}"
            sin="${sin}${sin:+, }$ses"
        done
        where="${where}${sin:+ and session in ($sin)}"
    fi

    local sep=$'\x1f'
    local orderdir='asc'
    local debug=0
    local opt=""
    for opt ($opts); do
        case $opt in
            --desc)
                orderdir='desc'
                ;;
            --sep*)
                sep=${opt#--sep}
                ;;
            --from*)
                local from=${opt#--from}
                case $from in
                    -*)
                        from="datetime('now', '$from')"
                        ;;
                    today)
                        from="datetime('now', 'start of day')"
                        ;;
                    yesterday)
                        from="datetime('now', 'start of day', '-1 day')"
                        ;;
                esac
                where="${where} and datetime(start_time, 'unixepoch') >= $from"
                ;;
            --status*)
                local xstatus=${opt#--status}
                case $xstatus in
                    <->)
                        where="${where} and exit_status = $xstatus"
                        ;;
                        error)
                        where="${where} and exit_status <> 0"
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            --until*)
                local until=${opt#--until}
                case $until in
                    -*)
                        until="datetime('now', '$until')"
                        ;;
                    today)
                        until="datetime('now', 'start of day')"
                        ;;
                    yesterday)
                        until="datetime('now', 'start of day', '-1 day')"
                        ;;
                esac
                where="${where} and datetime(start_time, 'unixepoch') <= $until"
                ;;
            -d)
                debug=1
                ;;
            --detail)
                cols="${cols}, exit_status, duration "
                selcols="${selcols}, exit_status as [?],duration as secs "
                ;;
            -h|--help)
                echo "$usage"
                return 0
                ;;
            --forget)
                forget=1
                ;;
            --yes)
                forget_accept=1
                ;;
            --exact)
                exact=1
                ;;
            --limit*)
                limit=${opt#--limit}
                ;;
        esac
    done

    if [[ -n "$*" ]]; then
        if [[ $exact -eq 0 ]]; then
            where="${where} and commands.argv glob '*$(sql_escape $@)*'"
        else
            where="${where} and commands.argv = '$(sql_escape $@)'"
        fi
    fi

    if [[ $forget -gt 0 ]]; then
        limit=""
    fi
    local seps=$(echo "$cols" | tr -c -d ',' | tr ',' $sep)
    cols="${cols}, replace(commands.argv, '
', '
$seps') as argv, max(start_time) as max_start"

    local mst="datetime(max_start, 'unixepoch')"
    local dst="datetime('now', 'start of day')"
    local timecol="strftime(case when $mst > $dst then '%H:%M' else '%d/%m' end, max_start, 'unixepoch', 'localtime') as time"

    selcols="${timecol}, ${selcols}, argv as cmd"

    local r_order="asc"
    if [[ $orderdir == "asc" ]]; then
        r_order="desc"
    fi

    local query="select ${selcols} from (select ${cols}
from
  commands
  join history on history.command_id = commands.id
  join places on history.place_id = places.id
where ${where}
group by history.command_id, history.place_id
order by max_start ${r_order}
${limit:+limit $limit}) order by max_start ${orderdir}"

    ## min max date?
    local count_query="select count(*) from (select ${cols}
from
  commands
  join history on history.command_id = commands.id
  join places  on history.place_id = places.id
where ${where}
group by history.command_id, history.place_id
order by max_start desc) order by max_start ${orderdir}"

    if [[ $debug = 1 ]]; then
        echo "$query"
    else
        local count=$(_histdb_query "$count_query")
        if [[ -p /dev/stdout ]]; then
            buffer() {
                ## this runs out of memory for big files I think perl -e 'local $/; my $stdin = <STDIN>; print $stdin;'
                temp=$(mktemp)
                cat >! "$temp"
                cat -- "$temp"
                rm -f -- "$temp"
            }
        else
            buffer() {
                cat
            }
        fi
        if [[ $sep == $'\x1f' ]]; then
            _histdb_query -header -separator $sep "$query" | iconv -f utf-8 -t utf-8 -c | buffer | "${HISTDB_TABULATE_CMD[@]}"
        else
            _histdb_query -header -separator $sep "$query" | buffer
        fi
        [[ -n $limit ]] && [[ $limit -lt $count ]] && echo "(showing $limit of $count results)"
    fi

    if [[ $forget -gt 0 ]]; then
        if [[ $forget_accept -gt 0 ]]; then
          REPLY=y
        else
          read -q "REPLY?Forget all these results? [y/n] "
        fi
        if [[ $REPLY =~ "[yY]" ]]; then
            _histdb_query "delete from history where
history.id in (
select history.id from
history
  left join commands on history.command_id = commands.id
  left join places on history.place_id = places.id
where ${where})"
            _histdb_query "delete from commands where commands.id not in (select distinct history.command_id from history)"
        fi
    fi
}
```
author: messaddek

title: [BUG] autoenv `deactivate` can corrupt the $PATH if plugins aren't loaded in the right order
body:
### Describe the bug

The order that plugins are loaded, particularly in relation to autoenv, is very important. For example:
```text
plugins(... autoenv ... common-aliases... brew ...)
```
will yield a very different result than:
```text
plugins(... brew ... autoenv ...)
```
I haven't exhaustively tested this, but I did solve my own issue with OMZ randomly breaking when leaving the autoenv-controlled area of my computer. I spent far too long tracking this down...



### Steps to reproduce

1. This may overall be the root cause: set your new terminal sessions to cd into the directory you're already in (e.g. if I open a new tab, that zsh session is automatically in the same directory as the one it came from).  
2. Set ` plugins=(git git-prompt autoenv brew common-aliases extract keychain pip postgres python rsync safe-paste thefuck universalarchive virtualenv nvm npm)`
3. Configure `.env` and `.env.leave` files to activate and deactivate a virtual environment however you normally do. I use a couple of wrapper utilities around my virtual environment management, so it's not really useful for me to show my exact files; but I can if it's useful.
4. Do `cd path/to/dir` so autoenv runs.
5. Do `cd ..`

At this point, you should notice that your session is broken. Themes don't work, etc, etc. Basic shell commands don't work. And lo and behold, you go to look at your `$PATH` and it's...wrong.

Now:
1. Set ` plugins=(brew git git-prompt common-aliases extract keychain pip postgres python rsync safe-paste thefuck universalarchive virtualenv nvm npm autoenv)` Note that `autoenv` is now at the end of the list.
2. Continue with the same steps otherwise as above.
3. When `cd ..` to leave the autoenv area...nothing breaks.

You check your `$PATH`, and it's perfectly intact!


### Expected behavior

Expected that the virtual environment doesn't get activated (and therefore specific environment variables get cached) before the session setup is complete. 

However, this may end up being just a configuration _nota bene_ rather than any major code re-design.

### Screenshots and recordings

If you need big dumps of `printenv` or something, happy to. But basically, the `homebrew` paths and others are missing from the `$PATH` variable after deactivation in the first scenario, and moving `autoenv` to the end of the plugins list fixes that. Other things are minorly different as well.

### OS / Linux distribution

MacOS 14.6.1

### Zsh version

5.9

### Terminal emulator

Terminal.app

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_

author: egeste

title: The git-commit plugin's implementation should use git-<command> scripts in $PATH rather than adding aliases
body:
### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I tried out the git-commit plugin, and it cluttered my `.gitconfig` with its aliases. When I stopped using the plugin, it had no way to clean up after itself. This is unexpected side-effect behavior for a plugin, and was frustrating. There's a better way to handle aliases like this.

### Describe the proposed feature.

Git supports running any script named `git-<command> "$@"` in you `$PATH` when you run `git <command> arg1 arg2...`. That means that the OMZ `git-commit` plugin could be simplified to something like:

```zsh
# git-commit.plugin.zsh
path=(${0:h}/commands $path)
```

And then add `git-foo` scripts into `$ZSH/plugins/git-commit/commands`. This would have a few benefits:

1. It's easier to maintain a real script [instead of a script string](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/2056aeeeaddd977eb205619c6f236b94dac896be/plugins/git-commit/git-commit.plugin.zsh#L32-L55), or inside of `.gitconfig`.
2. It's more canonical - rather than adding big shell scripted commands as large stringy aliases in `.gitconfig`, it's a commonly used git feature to add subcommands this way.
3. Because it's more canonical, it serves better to teach users how to add their own subcommands themselves. Many view OMZ as a teaching tool for their own config, and this hacky implementation isn't good for teaching best practices.
4. There's no cleanup needed. If you stop using the plugin, those commands stop being part of your `$PATH`.

Downsides:
1. Bash/Fish/other shells don't get the benefit of the side effects of having added `git-commit` in your Zsh config
2. The scripts need to be executable (`chmod u+x git-foo`), but that's not too difficult.

I am more than willing to submit a PR with a re-write that simplifies this plugin, but I don't want to do that without opening this issue first for feedback.

author: joe-chambers

title: Add a success/failure function to mvn plugin mvn-or-mvnw
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

mvn

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

When I run builds, especially long running builds I start another task.  I have put a success/failure validator around mvn such that it calls 'say "Build Complete." on success and "Build Failed." on failure.  It would be useful if there was a way to configure mvn-or-mvnw to be decorated with a mvn-success function or mvn-failure function so that custom actions could be performed when the build is complete.  Or at least a mvn-complete function.

### Describe the proposed feature.

A way to configure a method to be called after the mvn or mvnw call in mvn-or-mvnw.  I can see 2 ways of doing this but I have limited shell/zsh skills so maybe this is not doable.

1. add a decorator such as mvn-run around mvn-or-mvnw that accepts the return state and uses that to determine whether to call mvn-success or mvn-failure depending on whether it is defined, potentially an alias of some sort...
2. add a decorator such as mvn-run around mvn-or-mvnw that just calls mvn-complete depending on whether it is defined, potentially on an anlias of some sort...

It would be good if the mechanism gave the user some way of turning it off, like a quiet flag for running builds during meetings so that it doesn't go off while your manager is talking.


### Describe alternatives you've considered

I changed the mvn-or-mvnw function to directly make the changes I wanted, but this is intelligent at best and provides no mechanism for the customization for each individual developer.

### Additional context

_No response_

### Additional context

github_repo_data() {
    # 默认参数
    local repo="nushell/nushell"
    local version=""
    local latest=false
    local download_url=false
    local windows=false
    local linux=false
    local macos=false
    local token="$github_full_control_token"
    local help=false

    # 处理输入参数
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -Repo|--repo)
                repo="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -Version|--version)
                version="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -Latest|-n|--latest)
                latest=true
                shift
                ;;
            -DownloadUrl|-d|--download-url)
                download_url=true
                shift
                ;;
            -Windows|-w|--windows)
                windows=true
                shift
                ;;
            -Linux|-l|--linux)
                linux=true
                shift
                ;;
            -MacOS|-m|--macos)
                macos=true
                shift
                ;;
            -Token|--token)
                token="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -Help|--help)
                help=true
                shift
                ;;
            *)
                echo "未知参数: $1"
                return 1
                ;;
        esac
    done

    # 显示帮助信息
    if $help; then
        echo "使用说明"
        echo "该函数用于从 GitHub 获取指定仓库的发布信息和下载链接。"
        echo ""
        echo "## 示例用法"
        echo ""
        echo "# 获取 nushell 最新版本的所有发布信息"
        echo "github_repo_data"
        echo ""
        echo "# 获取 nushell 最新版本的 Windows 下载链接"
        echo "github_repo_data -Latest -DownloadUrl -Windows or github_repo_data -n -d -w"
        echo ""
        echo "# 获取 nushell 特定版本的 Linux 下载链接"
        echo "github_repo_data -Version '0.30.0' -DownloadUrl -Linux or github_repo_data -v '0.30.0' -d -l"
        return
    fi

    # 构建URL
    local url
    if [[ -n "$version" ]]; then
        url="https://api.github.com/repos/$repo/releases/tags/$version"
    elif $latest; then
        url="https://api.github.com/repos/$repo/releases/latest"
    else
        url="https://api.github.com/repos/$repo/releases"
    fi

    # 设置请求头，包含访问令牌
    local headers=(
        -H "Authorization: token $token"
        -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json"
    )

    # 获取JSON数据
    local json_data
    json_data=$(curl -s "${headers[@]}" "$url")
    # json_data=$(curl -s "${headers[@]}" "$url" | sed 's/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]//g')
    # json_data=$(curl -s "${headers[@]}" "$url" | tr -d '\000-\037\177')
    # BUG: oh-my-zsh -> jq: parse error: Invalid string: control characters from U+0000 through U+001F must be escaped at line 7, column 84

    # 调试输出
    # echo "$json_data" | head -n 10  # 显示前10行以检查数据

    # 如果需要下载链接
    if $download_url; then
        if $windows; then
            echo "$json_data" | jq -r '.assets[] | select(.browser_download_url | test(".*(pc|windows).*(zip|exe|msi)$")) | .browser_download_url'
        elif $linux; then
            echo "$json_data" | jq -r '.assets[] | select(.browser_download_url | test(".*(linux).*(deb|rpm|appimage|tar.gz)$")) | .browser_download_url'
        elif $macos; then
            echo "$json_data" | jq -r '.assets[] | select(.browser_download_url | test(".*(darwin|apple).*(dmg|pkg|tar.gz)$")) | .browser_download_url'
        else
            echo "$json_data" | jq -r '.assets[].browser_download_url'
        fi
    else
        echo "$json_data"
    fi
}

body: ### Describe the bug When attempting to SSH into an Ubuntu 20.04 server and after setting the default shell to zsh with Oh My Zsh, the terminal hangs indefinitely during the sourcing of the ``.zshrc`` file, specifically at the ``source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh`` line. This issue does not occur when Oh My Zsh is not installed or when .zshrc is removed. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Install `zsh` and Oh My Zsh on the server. 2. Configure `.zshrc` with minimal settings: ``` export ZSH=$HOME/.oh-my-zsh ZSH_THEME="robbyrussell" plugins=(git) source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh ``` 3. Attempt to SSH into the server or open the ``zsh``. 4. Observe that the terminal hangs without loading the shell. ### Expected behavior The terminal should load `zsh` with the specified Oh My Zsh configuration without hanging. ### Screenshots and recordings ### OS / Linux distribution Ubuntu 20.04 ### Zsh version 5.8 (x86_64-ubuntu-linux-gnu) ### Oh My Zsh version Latest ### Terminal emulator iTerm2, VS Code Terminal , cmd, PowerShell and more. ### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL None ### Additional context **Relevant logs and/or screenshots:** ``` +/home/zhao2z/.oh-my-zsh/oh-my-zsh.sh:11> [ -n 5.8 ']' # Protect against non-zsh execution of Oh My Zsh (use POSIX syntax here) ... +/home/zhao2z/.oh-my-zsh/oh-my-zsh.sh:43> [[+/home/zhao2z/.oh-my-zsh/oh-my-zsh.sh:43> emulate ``` Error-related outputs are indicative of an issue with the `emulate` function, which might be related to Oh My Zsh’s compatibility or a misconfiguration. I have checked related issues such as [[#11686](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/issues/11686)](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/issues/11686) but found that the problem seems unique to my setup as I have no explicit `emulate` settings.

author: MuadJOmar

title: Add zip file support to take function
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

take

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I use the `take` function pretty often to pull down archives or git repositories from remote servers. An issue I frequently encounter is that `take` doesn't support zip archives. So if I try to do `take https://site.com/path/to/file.zip`, `take` treats it as a directory and passes it to `takedir`, and then I'm left with a mess of directories in my current path, like `~/dev/http:/site.com/path/to/file.zip`. 

### Describe the proposed feature.

I'd like to add a `takezip` function to `lib/functions.zsh` that can handle zip files. After checking the `$1` for the https protocol and a tar/gz/bz2/xz etc extension, we'd add a similar `elif` that checks for a zip extension. If found, it calls `takezip` which then fetches the archive to a temp directory, unzips it, and moves the files to the working directory, just as `takeurl` works.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

### OS / Linux distribution

Official completion script:
```zsh
###-begin-pm2-completion-###
### credits to npm for the completion file model
#
# Installation: pm2 completion >> ~/.bashrc  (or ~/.zshrc)
#

COMP_WORDBREAKS=${COMP_WORDBREAKS/=/}
COMP_WORDBREAKS=${COMP_WORDBREAKS/@/}
export COMP_WORDBREAKS

if type complete &>/dev/null; then
  _pm2_completion () {
    local si="$IFS"
    IFS=$'\n' COMPREPLY=($(COMP_CWORD="$COMP_CWORD" \
                           COMP_LINE="$COMP_LINE" \
                           COMP_POINT="$COMP_POINT" \
                           pm2 completion -- "${COMP_WORDS[@]}" \
                           2>/dev/null)) || return $?
    IFS="$si"
  }
  complete -o default -F _pm2_completion pm2
elif type compctl &>/dev/null; then
  _pm2_completion () {
    local cword line point words si
    read -Ac words
    read -cn cword
    let cword-=1
    read -l line
    read -ln point
    si="$IFS"
    IFS=$'\n' reply=($(COMP_CWORD="$cword" \
                       COMP_LINE="$line" \
                       COMP_POINT="$point" \
                       pm2 completion -- "${words[@]}" \
                       2>/dev/null)) || return $?
    IFS="$si"
  }
  compctl -K _pm2_completion + -f + pm2
fi
###-end-pm2-completion-###
```

how to fix it, please visit the web page mentioned above.

--2024-10-10 10:29:35-- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh
Resolving raw.githubusercontent.com (raw.githubusercontent.com)... 198.18.0.198
Connecting to raw.githubusercontent.com (raw.githubusercontent.com)|198.18.0.198|:443... connected.
ERROR: The certificate of ‘raw.githubusercontent.com’ is not trusted.
ERROR: The certificate of ‘raw.githubusercontent.com’ doesn't have a known issuer.


I can indeed download normally using -k etc to bypass the ssl certificate check (which I did), but I'm still confused

author: RClaudiuM

title: _add_identities:16: regex matching error: illegal byte sequence
body:
### Describe the bug

After https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/pull/12741, I get this error on startup:
_add_identities:16: regex matching error: illegal byte sequence


### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable the ssh-agent plugin
2. Create a binary file in $HOME/.ssh (In my case, it's random_seed).
3. Start zsh

### Expected behavior

No errors.


### Screenshots and recordings

```
Last login: Sat Oct 12 11:35:31 on ttys001
_add_identities:16: regex matching error: illegal byte sequence
$ 
```

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 12.7.6

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Oh My Zsh version

master (d2d5155)

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

The issue seems to be that:
`   $(command head -n 1 "$file") =~ ^-----BEGIN\ OPENSSH\ PRIVATE\ KEY-----`
expects the output to be UTF-8, but the binary file is not UTF-8.

This diff fixes the issue for me, although I haven't tested with many key types:

```
diff --git a/plugins/ssh-agent/ssh-agent.plugin.zsh b/plugins/ssh-agent/ssh-agent.plugin.zsh
index becd5ea..14ab98d 100644
--- a/plugins/ssh-agent/ssh-agent.plugin.zsh
+++ b/plugins/ssh-agent/ssh-agent.plugin.zsh
@@ -45,7 +45,7 @@ function _add_identities() {
     # Iterate over files in .ssh folder.
     for file in "$HOME/.ssh"/*; do
       # Check if file is a regular file and starts with "-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----".
-      if [[ -f "$file" && $(command head -n 1 "$file") =~ ^-----BEGIN\ OPENSSH\ PRIVATE\ KEY----- ]]; then
+      if [[ -f "$file" && $(command head -n 1 "$file") = -----BEGIN\ OPENSSH\ PRIVATE\ KEY----- ]]; then
         # Add filename (without path) to identities array.
         identities+=("${file##*/}")
       fi
```

title: Autocorrect is doing it too much
body:
### Describe the bug

Hello

Your autocorrect is good, but it's also guessing too much. 

I have 4 datasets which are 2 VMs plus a zvol for the disks.

```
marabou/vm/npm-cali                            2.62G   905G   116K  /marabou/vm/npm-cali
marabou/vm/npm-cali-broken                     4.63G   905G   160K  /marabou/vm/npm-cali-broken
marabou/vm/npm-cali-broken/disk0               4.62G   905G  2.62G  -
marabou/vm/npm-cali/disk0                      2.62G   905G  2.62G  -

```

So I was moving the disk from `npm-cali-broken` to `npm-cali` and all of the disks on these VMs are called disk0 if there's one disk. title: External Theme - Heapbytes outdate info
body:
### Describe the bug

I installed the [Heapbytes Terminal](https://github.com/heapbytes/heapbytes-zsh) theme, which is one of the external themes listed on the [External Themes Wiki page](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/wiki/External-themes#heapbytes-terminal-theme), but I ran into two issues. One of the issue is that the prompt is showing a weird character, which could be a font issue but the wiki page mentioned not needing any external fonts: 

> **Features**
> - Fork/update/inspiration of rkj theme.
> - Prints the tun0 IP if connected to a VPN
> - Prints the wlan0 IP if you aren't connected to any VPN. (change the module name in .zsh-theme according to your wifi module)
> - Easy for pentesters who needs their ip for creating rev shells.
> - Git info.
> - Doesn't require any external fonts.

Second issue is that the `ls` command stopped working and this was returned: `zsh: command not found: exa`, which might be some issues with the alias. I tried downloading exa but it seems that [exa is no longer maintained and has been disabled by Homebrew](https://github.com/ogham/exa/issues/1243).

### Steps to reproduce

**Issue 1:**
Add the heapbytes-mac.zsh-theme file to my .oh-my-zsh/custom/themes folder
Updated ZSH_THEME="heapbytes" in the .zhrc file and included the changes seen in the .zshrc file for the heapbyte theme:
1. Updated plugins to include auto suggestion and highlights:
```
# Add wisely, as too many plugins slow down shell startup.
plugins=(git
  zsh-autosuggestions  
  zsh-syntax-highlighting
  )
```
2. Include other aliases and customization:
```
# On-demand rehash
zshcache_time="$(date +%s%N)"

autoload -Uz add-zsh-hook

rehash_precmd() {
  if [[ -a /var/cache/zsh/pacman ]]; then
    local paccache_time="$(date -r /var/cache/zsh/pacman +%s%N)"
    if (( zshcache_time < paccache_time )); then
      rehash
      zshcache_time="$paccache_time"
    fi
  fi
}

add-zsh-hook -Uz precmd rehash_precmd

# omz
alias zshconfig="geany ~/.zshrc"
alias ohmyzsh="thunar ~/.oh-my-zsh"

# exa --icons
# ls
alias ls='exa --icons'
alias l='exa --icons -lh'
alias ll='exa --icons -lah'
alias la='exa --icons -A'
alias lm='exa --icons -m'
alias lr='exa --icons -R'
alias lg='exa --icons -l --group-directories-first'

# git
alias gcl='git clone --depth 1'
alias gi='git init'
alias ga='git add'
alias gc='git commit -m'
alias gp='git push origin master'

#my

#alias nvim='$HOME/.local/bin/lvim'
#alias lvim='$HOME/.local/bin/lvim'
alias pinstall='sudo pacman -S'
alias upinstall='sudo pacman -Sy'
alias yinstall='yay -S'

precmd(){
	precmd(){
		echo 
	}
} 
```
Then restarted zsh with `exec zsh`

**Issue 2:**
Tried downloading exa with`brew install exa` 

### Expected behavior

As shown in the image in the wiki page, the strange character should be a triangle and the `ls` command should list out all the files in the current directory.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1233f0f6-d1a2-42ab-8e2f-3f520c920124)


### Screenshots and recordings

Strange character in place of a triangle and `ls` command not returning a list of files but: `zsh: command not found: exa`
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea7a3ce1-159e-4fc1-8b87-171a296e5f2c)
(I slightly tweaked the heapbytes-mac.zsh-theme file to not show my ip address.)

`brew install exa` showed that it is unmaintained and has been disabled:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2d580998-efe1-492b-b8e5-b569fc29d36c)



### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 12.6.7

### Zsh version

5.8.1

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2, Terminal.app

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

Hoping to update the info on the External Themes wiki page.

title: plugin: virtualenvwrapper doesn't cleanup properly after being disabled/reconfigured
body:
### Describe the bug

hello.
`virtualenvwrapper` plugin has this feature where it installs a zsh `chpwd` hook function named `workon_cwd`.
It has a configurable env variable for people like me: `DISABLE_VENV_CD`.
However if you define `export DISABLE_VENV_CD=1` and then source the plugin, it will leave previously defined function intact. :)

basically, when being sourced the plugin needs to check whether it needs the function and whether it is already present.

### Steps to reproduce

Assuming you have oh-my-zsh installed at default location.
 

## I. Enable the plugin `virtualenvwrapper` and see its operation:
```!zsh
$ omz plugin enable virtualenvwrapper
$ source ~/.zshrc
$ pushd "${PWD}"
$ cd /tmp
$ mkdir -p kuku
$ cd kuku
$ python3 -m venv .venv
$ cd ../kuku
(.venv) $ cd ../
$ popd
```
so far as expected.

## II. Disable cd handling:
```
$ export DISABLE_VENV_CD=1
$ source ~/.zshrc
$ cd /tmp/kuku
(.venv) $ 
```
i.e. the hook is still working :)

### Expected behavior

I expected at II last line I would not get any `chpw` hooks.


### Screenshots and recordings

don't forget to clean up:
```
rm -fr /tmp/kuku
```

### OS / Linux distribution

Mac OS 15.2

### Zsh version

5.9

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

## workarounds: 
1. use `omz reload`
2. manually clean up

## FIX:
add a test for DISABLE_VENV_CD undef or 0 and ensure that the hook ` is removed from `chpwd_functions`:
```
if [[ "${DISABLE_VENV_CD}" -eq 1 ]]; then
  autoload -U add-zsh-hook
  add-zsh-hook -d chpwd workon_cwd
fi
```

I know this is a rare use case and not a critical one.

author: grandpaslab

title: Git Tab Completion Not Working with Relative Paths
body:
### Describe the bug

I opened a [question on Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/q/79286373/7766024) and will just describe what I described there.

It seems like the autocompletion isn't working when I try to use relative path syntax.

For example, if I had the following project structure:

```
project/
├── server/
│   └── main.py
└── client.py
```

Then:

1. `git add cli` + <kbd>Tab</kbd> autocompletes to `git add client.py`.
2. `git add ./cli` + <kbd>Tab</kbd> does _not_ autocomplete to `git add ./client.py`.

---

I wrote this as being an Oh-My-Zsh bug because relative pathing works with all of the other commands, just not Git.

### Steps to reproduce

Try any Git command with no relative path vs. with a relative path.

### Expected behavior

Tab completion without relative pathing will autocomplete as expected. However, tab completion with relative pathing will not autocomplete.

title: [afowler] display caret color based on previous command exit code
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

afowler

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I made a comment in this [commit](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/commit/a649b2d410e3f3cd2b5749d199da03168d0c3920), but I thought I'd move it here for wider visibility and discussion (if any). 

There isn't a problem with this theme, I have been using it since whenever I started using ohmyzsh. I recently made a little tweak to this theme on my local .zshrc to display the caret colour based on the previous command exit code instead of "if I'm root, display red" as this theme currently does. 

As far as I could tell, once you enter sudo/privilege, your prompt changes to one of the following (if there isn't any other configuration): 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2ca48b83-2f92-4779-9f97-27c845bb657f)

Which isn't `afowler`'s prompt format, which means we don't really get to see the red caret.

### Describe the proposed feature.

I like to match the caret colour with the exit code displayed in RPS1 position, like so: 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2df860a7-cf38-45f5-8e2f-f1f7bcc6fdd9)

Blue caret for successfully executed command, which returns exit code 0
Red caret for failed command, which returns non-zero code

I thought this feels quite lovely.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

_No response_

author: cmon1701

title: Symbolic link for package manager plugins not working
body:
### Describe the bug

When installing a plugin via package manager, e.g. `dnf`, a symbolic link to the location of the package does not allow the import of the plugin.  I think it would be more convenient and clear, when all plugins are loaded via the `plugin` part of `.zshrc`. Additionally, installing (and updating) `zsh-syntax-highlighting` and `zsh-autosuggestions`  via a package manager is also more convenient than pulling them from git.

I am aware, that you could either install the plugin directly into `$ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins/` via 
```
git clone https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.git ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-~/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/zsh-syntax-highlighting
```
or `source` the files directly via 
```
echo "source /usr/share/zsh-syntax-highlighting/zsh-syntax-highlighting.zsh" >> ${ZDOTDIR:-$HOME}/.zshrc
echo "source /usr/share/zsh-autosuggestions/zsh-autosuggestions.zsh" >> ${ZDOTDIR:-$HOME}/.zshrc
```

### Steps to reproduce

- Install `zsh` and custom plugins via package manager
```
sudo dnf install zsh zsh-autosuggestions zsh-syntax-highlighting
```
- Install oh-my-zsh 
```
sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"
```
- Create symbolic link 
```
ln -s /usr/share/zsh-autosuggestions $ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions
```
- Loading plugins via plugin manager
```
cat ~/.zshrc | grep -i plugin
# Which plugins would you like to load?
# Standard plugins can be found in $ZSH/plugins/
# Custom plugins may be added to $ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins/
# Example format: plugins=(rails git textmate ruby lighthouse)
# Add wisely, as too many plugins slow down shell startup.
plugins=(git zsh-autosuggestions)
```
- Verify the symbolic link
```
ls -l $ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 kevin kevin 30 Jan  9 20:03 /home/kevin/.oh-my-zsh/custom/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions -> /usr/share/zsh-autosuggestions
```
- Load `zsh`
```
source ~/.zshrc 
[oh-my-zsh] plugin 'zsh-autosuggestions' not found
```

### Expected behavior

I would expect that the symbolic link does resolve and the plugin can be loaded.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Fedora Linux 41 (Workstation Edition)

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (5c17bcd)

### Terminal emulator

GNOME Terminal

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_

```
# load a random theme each time Oh My Zsh is loaded, in which case,
# to know which specific one was loaded, run: echo $RANDOM_THEME

# a theme from this variable instead of looking in $ZSH/themes/

# zstyle ':omz:update' mode auto      # update automatically without asking

# Uncomment the following line to change how often to auto-update (in days).
# zstyle ':omz:update' frequency 13

# Uncomment the following line to enable command auto-correction.
# ENABLE_CORRECTION="true"

# Uncomment the following line to display red dots whilst waiting for completion.
# You can also set it to another string to have that shown instead of the default red dots.
# e.g. COMPLETION_WAITING_DOTS="%F{yellow}waiting...%f"
# Caution: this setting can cause issues with multiline prompts in zsh < 5.7.1 (see #5765)
# COMPLETION_WAITING_DOTS="true"

# Uncomment the following line if you want to disable marking untracked files
# under VCS as dirty. This makes repository status check for large repositories
# much, much faster.

# Uncomment the following line if you want to change the command execution time
# stamp shown in the history command output.

# Would you like to use another custom folder than $ZSH/custom?
# ZSH_CUSTOM=/path/to/new-custom-folder

# Which plugins would you like to load?
# Standard plugins can be found in $ZSH/plugins/
# Custom plugins may be added to $ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins/
# Example format: plugins=(rails git textmate ruby lighthouse)
# Add wisely, as too many plugins slow down shell startup.
plugins=(git)

source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh

# User configuration

# export MANPATH="/usr/local/man:$MANPATH"

# You may need to manually set your language environment
# export LANG=en_US.UTF-8

# Preferred editor for local and remote sessions
# if [[ -n $SSH_CONNECTION ]]; then
#   export EDITOR='vim'
# else
#   export EDITOR='nvim'
# fi

# Compilation flags
# export ARCHFLAGS="-arch $(uname -m)"

# Set personal aliases, overriding those provided by Oh My Zsh libs,
# plugins, and themes. Aliases can be placed here, though Oh My Zsh
# users are encouraged to define aliases within a top-level file in
# the $ZSH_CUSTOM folder, with .zsh extension. Examples:
# - $ZSH_CUSTOM/aliases.zsh

# For a full list of active aliases, run `alias`.
#
# Example aliases
# alias zshconfig="mate ~/.zshrc"
# alias ohmyzsh="mate ~/.oh-my-zsh"

autoload -Uz compinit
compinit
``` 

author: dis0908

title: Using oh-my-zsh and per-directory-history remove $1 from preexec_functions
body:
### Describe the bug

The original problem is [atuin not working with per-directory-history plugin](https://github.com/atuinsh/atuin/issues/1892).

Atuin hooks into the preexec hook to store the command in a sqlite database to have an _improved_ history.

When per-directory-history plugin is enabled, via oh-my-zsh, the function configured in preexec_functions by atuin does not receive the "$1" with the command executed.

In the _steps to reproduce_ I show the problem, using podman to start with a new alpine linux.

Using per-directory-history plugin without oh-my-zsh (directly _sourcing_ it) works correctly.

If I comment the `fc -p $_per_directory_history_directory` line, and source the plugin again, the hook works correctly (but not the plugin):
```bash
function _per-directory-history-addhistory() {
  # respect hist_ignore_space
  if [[ -o hist_ignore_space ]] && [[ "$1" == \ * ]]; then
      true
  else
      print -Sr -- "${1%%$'\n'}"
      # instantly write history if set options require it.
      if [[ -o share_history ]] || \
         [[ -o inc_append_history ]] || \
         [[ -o inc_append_history_time ]]; then
          fc -AI $HISTFILE
          fc -AI $_per_directory_history_directory
      fi
      #fc -p $_per_directory_history_directory
  fi
}
```

### Steps to reproduce

```bash
❯ podman run -it --rm alpine
/ # apk add zsh git
fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.20/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.20/community/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz

(5/18) Installing libidn2 (2.3.7-r0)
(6/18) Installing nghttp2-libs (1.62.1-r0)
(7/18) Installing libpsl (0.21.5-r1)
(8/18) Installing zstd-libs (1.5.6-r0)
(9/18) Installing libcurl (8.11.1-r0)
(10/18) Installing libexpat (2.6.4-r0)
(11/18) Installing pcre2 (10.43-r0)
(12/18) Installing git (2.45.3-r0)
(13/18) Installing git-init-template (2.45.3-r0)
(14/18) Installing libcap2 (2.70-r0)
(15/18) Installing ncurses-terminfo-base (6.4_p20240420-r2)
(16/18) Installing libncursesw (6.4_p20240420-r2)
(17/18) Installing zsh (5.9-r3)
Executing zsh-5.9-r3.post-install
(18/18) Installing git-zsh-completion (5.9-r3)
Executing busybox-1.36.1-r29.trigger
Executing ca-certificates-20241121-r1.trigger
OK: 28 MiB in 32 packages
/ # git clone https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.git ~/.oh-my-zsh
Cloning into '/root/.oh-my-zsh'...
remote: Enumerating objects: 33990, done.
remote: Counting objects: 100% (4230/4230), done.

remote: Total 33990 (delta 3977), reused 3892 (delta 3892), pack-reused 29760 (from 4)
Receiving objects: 100% (33990/33990), 11.08 MiB | 28.29 MiB/s, done.
Resolving deltas: 100% (19023/19023), done.
/ # zsh
131e813718c8#

131e813718c8# function foo(){ echo "\$1: $1    \$2: $2" }; set -A preexec_functions foo

131e813718c8# pwd

$1: pwd
    $2: pwd
/
131e813718c8# echo "works ok"

$1: echo "works ok"
    $2: echo "works ok"
works ok
```

author: jtm13jm

title: A new command for quickly adding a directory to the PATH variable
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

Add to path

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Adding directory paths to the PATH environment variable is one of the most common operations, but is often fraught with errors. It's too easy to mistype a directory path and then wondering why a particular command doesn't work.  

### Describe the proposed feature.

It would be nice if there was an OMZ command for performing it. It could work like so:

add_to_path [_directory_] [**_shell_** | _global_ | _login_]

_directory_                    Specifies a directory to add to the path. Current directory is the default. Autocomplete can be used
_shell_            The path set is whenever an interactive shell is opened. This is the default.
_global_        The path is set globally for all users
_login_           The path is set when current user logs in

The changes are made in the appropriate configuration files.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

None.

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

_No response_

author: mahendrakariya

title: Soliah theme reports head in "detached-head" state at all times
body:
### Describe the bug

Using the Soliah theme and latest version of omz, I don't get the branch on the prompt anymore. Instead, I always see "detached-head" state. It looks like git_prompt_info function has been adjusted to use:
```
git_prompt_info () {
        if [[ -n "${_OMZ_ASYNC_OUTPUT[_omz_git_prompt_info]}" ]]
        then
                echo -n "${_OMZ_ASYNC_OUTPUT[_omz_git_prompt_info]}"
        fi
}
```

and that appears to be the function used by the theme. However, I get an empty value for that prompt always, and this causes the theme to believe its detached head. 

If tried using:

title: Compinit runs every time I open shell which slows it down
body:
### Describe the bug

I noticed my shell being slow starting up. After debugging source files I found that the problem was in the oh my zsh source file and that the line that initializes the completion system is the culprit `compinit -i -d "$ZSH_COMPDUMP"`.
This compinit shouldn't run unless the OMZ zcompdump metadata changed, but the `$fpath`  is different everytime.
I didn't go as far as debugging  why is it different, but I figured it could be just a difference in order or duplication of paths and I was kind of right because adding `fpath=(${(uo)fpath})` fixed the problem:
```bash
fpath=(${(uo)fpath})

# Construct zcompdump OMZ metadata
zcompdump_revision="#omz revision: $(builtin cd -q "$ZSH"; git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
zcompdump_fpath="#omz fpath: $fpath"
...
```

I ll provide the fpath metadata both from the zcompdump and echo from the source script
`oh-my-zsh.sh`:
```bash
...
# Construct zcompdump OMZ metadata
zcompdump_revision="#omz revision: $(builtin cd -q "$ZSH"; git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
zcompdump_fpath="#omz fpath: $fpath"

echo $zcompdump_fpath
...
```

title: Show a message instead of a prompt on startup
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

I think that's a generic behavior.


### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Users of this software usually fire up a terminal and start typing instantly.
And sometimes, a prompt shows up when you open a new terminal and asks for an input

`Would you like to update? [y/n]`

Most of the time, we skip that prompt by pressing an invalid button (because we expect a command input instead of a dialog)
Not a big deal. But this creates a small small friction. 


### Describe the proposed feature.

If we replace the prompt with a bold fonted message to say "It's been a while, you may want to run `omz update` if you want your shell updated". People can ignore that message as long as they want. 

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I can not think any other alternative.

title: Enhance Azure Plugin Prompt with Color-coded Subscription Info
body: ### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here. Azure Plugin ### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here. The Azure CLI plugin's prompt lacked visual distinction between different components (subscription ID and tenant), making it harder to quickly identify which Azure environment you're working in. The monochrome output made it difficult to parse the information at a glance. ### Describe the proposed feature. 1. Breaking down the information into clearly separated, color-coded sections: - Azure symbol (𝚫) in deep blue - Subscription ID in medium blue - Tenant name in light blue 2. Adding color gradients to distinguish between different components 3. Using different shades of blue to maintain a professional Azure-themed look ``` function azure_prompt_info() { # Check for azure profile and jq availability local azure_profile="${AZURE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.azure}/azureProfile.json" [[ ! -f "${azure_profile}" ]] && return (( $+commands[jq] )) || return 1 # Get current subscription info local sub_info sub_info=$(jq -r '.subscriptions[] | select(.isDefault==true) | "\(.id | split("-")[0]):\(.tenantDefaultDomain | split(".")[0])"' "${azure_profile}") [[ -z "${sub_info}" ]] && return # Split subscription info into components local subscription_id="${sub_info%%:*}" local tenant_name="${sub_info#*:}" # Format with colors local azure_symbol="%F{33}𝚫%f" local subscription_part="%F{39}${subscription_id}%f" local tenant_part="%F{45}${tenant_name}%f" echo "${ZSH_THEME_AZURE_PREFIX:=(}${azure_symbol}|${subscription_part}:${tenant_part}${ZSH_THEME_AZURE_SUFFIX:=)}" } ``` **Before** **After** ### Describe alternatives you've considered _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_ ### Related Issues _No response_

author: mhalano

title: Add search functionality for git aliases
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

Git plugin

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

I'd like to propose adding a new alias to the git plugin that allows users to search through existing git aliases. This would make it much easier to discover and remember available git shortcuts without having to look through the plugin source code.


### Describe the proposed feature.


**Proposed Implementation:**

Add a new alias called galias (git alias search) that would allow searching through existing git aliases with an optional search term.
Example usage:
```
# List all git aliases
galias

# Search for aliases containing 'fetch'
galias fetch
```

**Implementation Details:**
The implementation could be as simple as:
```
# In the git plugin file (git.plugin.zsh)
alias galias='_galias_search'

_galias_search() {
  local search_term="$1"
  if [[ -z "$search_term" ]]; then
    # If no search term provided, list all git aliases
    alias | grep "='git" | sort
  else
    # If search term provided, filter aliases containing that term
    alias | grep "='git" | grep "$search_term" | sort
  fi
}
```

**Benefits:**

Improves discoverability of existing git aliases
Helps users remember specific aliases for git commands
Follows the Unix philosophy of making tools that do one thing well
Consistent with Oh My Zsh's focus on improving productivity



### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

This feature would be particularly helpful for new users who are learning the available shortcuts, as well as experienced users who want to quickly find the right alias without having to remember all of them.

### Related Issues

_No response_

title: Support $ZSH on a noexec-mounted partition
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

_No response_

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

If the OMZ's main directory is on a partition with noexec set, `omz update` partially fails - I think the main update part passes, it only fails to print out the changelog.

I'm hitting this specifically on a TrueNAS box, as since upgrading to 24.10.1+ the noexec flag was fixed in ZFS - ref discussion on truenas forums: https://forums.truenas.com/t/shell-script-permission-denied-with-24-10-1/27941

### Describe the proposed feature.

Calling all sub-scripts via a `zsh <script>` instead of directly should solve the issue.

In particular changing the `$ZSH/tools/changelog.sh` calls in `tools/upgrade.sh:257` and `lib/cli.zsh:196` should mitigate the observed `omz update` partially failing.

I've additionally grepped the OMZ directory and found no other suspicious direct script calls, but my grep-fu might be weak here.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

### Related Issues

author: Mikrz

title: alias-finder: "grep: invalid repetition count(s)" for long command
body:
### Describe the bug

## Symptom

alias-finder gets an error for the long command.

```sh
❯❯❯ export TEXT=$(printf '1%.0s' {1..256})
❯❯❯ alias-finder --cheaper $TEXT # created filter is `^'?.{1,255}'?=`
# no error

❯❯❯ export TEXT=$(printf '1%.0s' {1..257})
❯❯❯ alias-finder --cheaper $TEXT # created filter is `^'?.{1,256}'?=`
grep: invalid repetition count(s)
```

## Cause

This happens because of the filter query for `--cheaper` option:

https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/6414a66e71e77bea0633340a6865a649d25a1b2e/plugins/alias-finder/alias-finder.plugin.zsh#L43

Specifically, it's `grep` problem. `grep` can't hold 256 length regex (I couldn't find related code yet): 

```sh
$ grep --version
grep (BSD grep, GNU compatible) 2.6.0-FreeBSD
```

This is not alias-finder's fault, but I think we can improve user experience to reduce frustration for this part. 


Further investigation is needed, like:

- Do `egrep` or other grep family behave the same way?
- Do other grep distributions or versions behave the same way?
- Do all OS behave the same way?
- Can we change some option so that grep can hold it?

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable plugin `alias-finder`
2. Manually execute the plugin with `--cheaper` and a long command. The total length othe f command should be longer than 256.

### Expected behavior

- Successfully process grep filtering.

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

macOS 15.3.2

### Zsh version

5.9

### Terminal emulator

iTerm2

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_

title: GitLab CLI Plugin request
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here. GitLab CLI - `glab` ### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here. Currently, `oh-my-zsh` does not include native support for the [GitLab CLI](https://docs.gitlab.com/editor_extensions/gitlab_cli/) (`glab`). I use `glab` regularly to interact with GitLab from the terminal, and it's quite helpful for managing MRs, issues, pipelines, etc. However, I have to manually configure completions and aliases in my `.zshrc`. A plugin would improve usability, reduce duplication, and provide a standard setup for users across environments. ### Describe the proposed feature. Add an `oh-my-zsh` plugin for `glab` that: - Enables shell completion automatically. - Optionally sets some useful aliases that are GitLab specific or `glab` related. - Optionally detects and sets `GITLAB_HOST` and `GITLAB_TOKEN` from `.netrc` (or document how to do so).
My current .zshrc setup looks like this. ```zsh function __parse_netrc() { local netrc="$1" local searched_host="$2" local host user token while IFS= read -r line; do case $line in machine*) host=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}') ;; *login*) user=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}') ;; *password*) token=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}') if [[ $host == *"$searched_host"* ]]; then echo "$host $user $token" return fi ;; esac done <"$netrc" # This should only happen if the searched host is not # found or the .netrc file is malformed return 1 } function __set_gitlab_env() { local netrc="$1" if [[ -f "$netrc" ]]; then read -r host user token < <(__parse_netrc "$netrc" "gitlab.devops.telekom.de") export GITLAB_HOST="https://$host" export GITLAB_USER="$user" export GITLAB_TOKEN="$token" export GITLAB_API_TOKEN="$token" else echo 'No .netrc file found at $(realpath "$netrc"). Please create one with your GitLab credentials.' return 1 fi } # glab is a GitLab CLI: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/cli if command -v glab &>/dev/null; then __set_gitlab_env "$HOME/.netrc" || { echo "Failed to set GitLab environment variables. Please check your .netrc file." } source <(glab completion -s zsh) compdef _glab glab alias gpsci='git push -o ci.skip' __glab_clone() { glab repo clone -g "$1" -a=false -p --paginate "${@:2}"; } alias glab-clone='__glab_clone' fi unset -f __parse_netrc unset -f __set_gitlab_env ```
### Describe alternatives you've considered Keeping my manual setup in `.zshrc` (shown above), but it feels like this could benefit more people. Additionally it'd naturally extend the plugins since the [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) `gh` is already [included](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/tree/750d3ac4b493dca13ef0ced55fa6a2cd02dc7ee8/plugins/gh). ### Additional context _No response_ ### Related Issues _No response_

author: wizardishungry

title: Conflict between `Esc` key bindings for `tldr-command-line` and `sudo-command-line`
body:
### Describe the bug

In the Zsh configuration, the `Esc` key is bound to trigger two different functions:  
1. The `tldr-command-line` function, which allows users to quickly view a command's `tldr` help page.
2. The `sudo-command-line` function, triggered by pressing `Esc` twice (`Esc Esc`), which adds `sudo` as a prefix to the command.

### Steps to reproduce

Pressing the `Esc` key and then pressing the `T`, `L`, `D`, and `R` keys in turn will have no effect.

Although double-clicking the `Esc` key triggers `sudo-command-line`.

### Expected behavior

**`tldr-command-line`** will be triggered with **`Esc`** + **`T`**, avoiding conflicts with `sudo-command-line`.

**`sudo-command-line`** will continue to work with **`Esc`** + **`Esc`**, adding the `sudo` prefix to the command.

Users will be able to quickly access the `tldr` help pages using **`Esc`** + **`T`**, while also retaining the ability to use `sudo` functionality with **`Esc`** + **`Esc`**.

### Screenshots and recordings

author: TullyMonster

title: asdf plugin fails to initialize when asdf is installed locally in ~/.asdf
body:
### Describe the bug

After the recent update ([2 weeks ago](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/a78d006faa022a5104b542d7dec5a3439e72046e/plugins/asdf/asdf.plugin.zsh) ) removing support for legacy <0.16 asdf setups, the plugin now short-circuits with:


```bash
(( ! $+commands[asdf] )) && return
```

This causes the plugin to exit before setting $PATH or completions, even if the user has asdf properly installed in ~/.asdf, which is the standard install path per official docs.

This breaks valid setups where .asdf is not sourced globally (e.g., via .zshrc) but expected to work via plugin loading.

Expected behavior: if .asdf directory exists, the plugin should initialize asdf even if not yet in $PATH.

Proposed fix: Replace the early command check with a check for the install directory, e.g.:

```bash
if [[ ! -d "$HOME/.asdf" ]]; then
  return
fi
```

At least it is working for me. I am not expert for oh my zsh plugins but when I change this, it is working fine on my end.

### Steps to reproduce

1. install asdf
2. enable asdf plugin with oh my zsh
3. try run `asdf --version`

### Expected behavior

Should initialize asdf without issue

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS

author: muhammedogz

title: "Branch" plugin displays only the last portion of branch name if it includes forward slash "/"
body:
### Describe the bug If the branch name includes forward slash "/", ohmyzsh plugin [branch](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/tree/master/plugins/branch) deletes the contents of the `.git/HEAD` until the last forward slash and then displays the resulting string as the branch. This method outputs wrong branch name if the branch name itself contains the character "/". See https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/plugins/branch/branch.plugin.zsh#L11: ```bash # Branch: displays the current Git or Mercurial branch fast. # Victor Torres # Oct 2, 2015 function branch_prompt_info() { # Start checking in current working directory local branch="" dir="$PWD" while [[ "$dir" != '/' ]]; do # Found .git directory if [[ -d "${dir}/.git" ]]; then branch="${"$(<"${dir}/.git/HEAD")"##*/}" # Delete until last "/" echo '±' "${branch:gs/%/%%}" return fi ... # Check parent directory dir="${dir:h}" done } ``` ### Steps to reproduce 1. Clone a git repository or create a new one. 2. Create a branch with a name that includes forward slash "/" 3. Switch to that branch 4. The ["branch"](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/750d3ac4b493dca13ef0ced55fa6a2cd02dc7ee8/plugins/branch/branch.plugin.zsh#L11) plugin doesn't show the full branch name but only shows the last portion of the branch name(if we assume branch name is separated by "/").
For example instead of `some/branch` it shows `branch` ```bash $ git clone https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.git Cloning into 'ohmyzsh'... remote: Enumerating objects: 34257, done. remote: Counting objects: 100% (78/78), done. remote: Compressing objects: 100% (46/46), done. remote: Total 34257 (delta 64), reused 32 (delta 32), pack-reused 34179 (from 5) Receiving objects: 100% (34257/34257), 12.23 MiB | 5.44 MiB/s, done. Resolving deltas: 100% (18268/18268), done. $ cd ohmyzsh $ git branch --copy master some/branch $ git switch some/branch Switched to branch 'some/branch' Your branch is up to date with 'origin/master'. ``` ### Expected behavior The "branch" plugin to show the full path, i.e. `some/branch` instead of `branch` ### Screenshots and recordings _No response_ ### OS / Linux distribution Arch Linux ### Zsh version 5.9 ### Oh My Zsh version master ### Terminal emulator kitty ### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL None ### Additional context _No response_

title: Bug in Emacs ANSI Term tracking in Emacs plugin
body:
### Describe the bug

When using the emacs plugin, when I launch vterm or multivterm in Emacs it prints out the characters `nSiTu` and my username, and `nSiTc` and the directory. Whenever I change directory, it also prints the directory part. 

Example:

```
nSiTc /home/username
nSiTu username
➜  ~ cd Downloads
nSiTc /home/username/Downloads
➜  Downloads cd ..
nSiTc /home/username
➜  ~ 
```

This appears to be coming from [here](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/a84a0332a822a78ddf3f66d0e1ed3990d4badd12/plugins/emacs/emacs.plugin.zsh#L37). Someone smarter than me on reddit suggested that this feature should only be used in `term` buffers, and that this plugin should detect which kind of buffer is being used and act accordingly.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable plugin `emacs`
2. Launch emacs
3. Launch `vterm` or `multivterm` buffer

title: Very slow response on git repository.
body:
### Describe the bug

I'm using ohmyzsh in iTerm2 of Mac.

I'm suffered very slow response while entering the directory cloned with git repository. 
Even if I don't use git plugin, using a theme that shows a git status in the prompt response very slowly.
And some completion that seems to read git branches, such as type 'git checkout de' and then press tab, it is stopping for a few seconds.

Below is part of the results measured by zprof;
```
num  calls                time                       self            name
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 1) 689855       410721.78     0.60   64.90%  410721.78     0.60   64.90%  (anon) [:2457]
 2) 689859       534923.52     0.78   84.53%  124200.65     0.18   19.63%  @zinit-scheduler
 3)   32       58937.22  1841.79    9.31%  35685.10  1115.16    5.64%  VCS_INFO_get_data_git
```

The response is fast on the home computer, only on the machine in my office, which installs the security softwares that audit the network and storages. Does the tab completion or dirty check always lookup remote repository, or just local repository? Is there any option to turn off the git lookup?

### Steps to reproduce

title: extract to another directory
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

extract

### Describe the proposed feature.

```console
$ extract
Usage: extract [-option] [file ...]

Options:
    -r, --remove    Remove archive after unpacking.
```



The extract command only extracts archives into the current directory. Please add an option so that we can extract archives into a different directory.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

An alternative way is to move or copy the archive into the desired directory, then extract it. But it's inconvenient.

title: Debian Plugin - $apt_pref not used when sudo isn't present
body:
### Describe the bug

Hi,

I have a Debian system where I don't have sudo installed, nor do I have aptitude.

When I do a "adu" I get the following:

```
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree... Done
Reading state information... Done
All packages are up to date.
zsh:1: command not found: aptitude
```

Linie 86 and 87 appear to have `aptitude` hardcoded.

Changing these two lines to have `$apt_pref` instead fixes the issue for me.

### Steps to reproduce

Add "debian" to your plugin list on a system that

a) Does not have sudo installed
b) Does not have aptitude installed

author: jhomer-hscl

title: Consolidate symfony, symfony2, and symfony6 plugins into a single Symfony plugin
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

This request is for the `symfony`, `symfony2`, and `symfony6` plugins.

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Currently, Symfony users are forced to choose between three separate plugins for the same framework (`symfony`, `symfony2`, and `symfony6`), which can be confusing and redundant. It also increases maintenance overhead and causes duplicated functionality.

### Describe the proposed feature.

I propose consolidating the three existing Symfony-related plugins into a single unified `symfony` plugin that:
 - Supports aliases that are common across all Symfony versions (e.g., `sf` → `bin/console`)
 - Uses logic to detect Symfony version if needed (e.g., checking for `app/console` or `bin/console`)
 - Retains useful features from `symfony2` and `symfony6` as appropriate
 - Is actively maintained in one place instead of fragmenting effort
This change would provide a cleaner, more user-friendly experience for all Symfony developers, regardless of the version they use.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

An alternative would be to deprecate the outdated `symfony` plugin (last updated 7 years ago) and encourage the use of only `symfony6`. However, this excludes developers working with Symfony 4 or 5, and still splits functionality. A single unified plugin offers a better long-term solution.

### Additional context

The `symfony` plugin hasn’t been updated in 7 years, while `symfony2` and `symfony6` provide overlapping functionality. Consolidating them would follow Oh My Zsh’s own contribution guideline to avoid duplicating functionality and to ensure plugins are useful to a broad number of users.

cc @robbyrussell for visibility, as the last known committer to these plugins.

### Related Issues

No related open or closed issues were found at the time of writing.
author: Hanashiko

title: Consolidate laravel, laravel4, and laravel5 plugins into a single Laravel plugin
body:
### If the feature request is for a plugin or theme, specify it here.

This request is for the laravel, laravel4, and laravel5 plugins.

### If the feature solves a problem you have, specify it here.

Currently, Laravel users must choose between three separate plugins—laravel, laravel4, and laravel5—despite their overlapping functionality. This redundancy can cause confusion, increase maintenance costs, and fragment contributions across similar codebases.

### Describe the proposed feature.

I propose merging these three Laravel-related plugins into a single, unified laravel plugin that:
 - Supports common Laravel aliases and helper commands across all major versions (e.g., artisan, tinker, serve)
 - Detects Laravel version-specific behavior where necessary
 - Retains unique or valuable functionality from laravel4 and laravel5
 - Consolidates maintenance into a single plugin to streamline updates and avoid duplication
 - Follows the Oh My Zsh principle of reducing plugin sprawl for similar technologies
This would provide a more consistent and user-friendly experience for Laravel developers regardless of project version.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

One option would be to deprecate laravel4 and laravel5, maintaining only the laravel plugin. However, this risks losing functionality specific to older Laravel versions and still leaves fragmentation in how Laravel projects are supported. A unified plugin is a more robust and long-term solution.

### Additional context

The current split between these three plugins reflects historical Laravel versioning, but no longer aligns with modern usage patterns. Combining them would simplify onboarding and reduce duplication.
cc @carlosala as the most recent editor of these plugins for visibility and input.

### Related Issues

No related open or closed issues were found at the time of writing.
